When I was growing up, tablets lap-tops and cell phones didn't exist. To search any information like address or phone number we had to use "Minitel", there was one phone per home and the computer was considered like a luxe item. In the late 90's, appeared the first cell phones and computers. I recall that the both were really heavies, also internet connection was totally unknown. Over 10 years, we've discovered more and more kinds of media, the cell phones became lighters and smarters. Computers have been replaced by lap-tops. In the early 2000's , is arrived a huge wave of technology: Tablets, smart phones, internet connection, on-demand TV, HD TV. Nowadays, you can get any information anytime; anywhere; where you want; where you need.  It's so easy now to get access to internet, it's really difficulft to imagine how could we do in the past? This technology is totally integrated into our daily life, we can't live without it.

When I was young, to keep myself informed about the news, I had three possibilities: read the newspapers, watch television or listen to the radio. The most common way was watching TV because we had both: the informations and the images of the events. But, since the early 2000s, a new genre of media appears: Internet. At first, it was like the newspapers, we only had informations and some pictures to illustrate them; but as time went by, as the internet network improved, so today we can watch the news online at any time, and everywhere. In fact, tablets, e-readers and smartphones are more and more widespread, so it's easier to read a book while commuting or while we are in a waiting room. Digital media has taken a huge place in our life, it's still improving every year, so I wouldn't be surprised if, unfortunately, someday the former mass media like newspapers would disappear.

In France, even nowadays ,table manners at the dinner table are important.And generally ,etiquette still matters.This applies more strictly when you are the member of high ,aristocratic society.The manners of simple folks are more unconventionnal,more "functional".But,for example, if an olderly person gets on the bus,offer your seat -is a normal gesture.If you're invited ,it's normal take a small gift with you (particularly if the host has kids- a little toy ):a bottle of wine,a bouquet of flowers,a box of chocolates...If you make phone calls or send sms messages while you are at the table,these considered as bad manners.It's very important that manners have to do with showing respect toward others,not only for "ceremony".

I was happy and everything in my life is hapiness but I work very hard to obtain what I want. Last year I began my study at the university to obtain my licence. It was very hard and difficult for me and my familly and mainly with my work. But after a lot of work and studies I obtain my licence and now I register to a master at the university. I'am very afraid because I will leave my job and begin a new life of student. My daughters are big and they can be independant. I advice for all people If you want to do or realize something you can if you put all your willpower to success and obtain what do you want. It's possible like me to begin a new life for a best life.

I don't have a real stressful life: I used to be an event planner for a restaurant and a club in Paris which belongs to a group of several places. I was working 35 to 40 hours a week, which is the regular working hours in France and I was often abroad on long lasting week ends. But, as a consequence, I was living from paycheck to paycheck, in fact you can't have everything : my apartment renting was high, the cost of living is also expensive in Paris and everytime I went out of Paris it cost almost 10% of your income. My social life was also amazing: I went out 2 or 3 times a week: as a PR for a famous place in Paris, you have to taste other restaurants, visit new places, go to the famous events to be seen and make new contacts... It cost a lot of money and was harassing sometimes when you just want to stay home in front of a TV show!

topic: Buying a painting for a friend
text: Leadership qualities could be a quite large topic to be discussed! Even if you could learn some technics through a training, according to me, the core of leadership is innate and linked with your personality and charisma. That's why there is so many different types of leaderships. We are all sensitive to different aspects of human relationship; this will also affect our viewpoint on management. However, I think we can define some common characteristics for leadership qualities. From my side, I would say that charisma, empathy, humor, self-confidence and equity and smile are important to develop a good leadership. If you are scared by talking in public in front of a few people or if you act like a real tyrant, I don'y think you will be considered as a good leader. On the other hand, if you are too friendly, you won't be respected. Somehow, owning leadership is a subtle mix of sweetness and fimness...

When I was younger, the most prominent media were: newspapers,radio and television. Now, I would say that it is has been a huge change in the way we are consuming media. Most people nowadays, especially teenagers are making use of internet to obtain the information they desire at the cost of television and radio. This evolution has been facilitated by the emergence of new technological devices such as: tablets,smartphones and laptops. To my mind, I believe that the change in the way media are using is mainly due to the fact that radio and television are becoming old-fashioned. Indeed on the internet you can choose what kind of information you intent to read or see and not just "undergo" the news like on television. Traditional media will need to adapt their tv shows if they do not want to lose viewers. An example could be the integration of tweets during their program in order to involve the viewer in the program show.

This article aims at people who need help in transferring to a more gratifying job. The author provides the reader with a two-step startegy enabling him to achieve his dream job.  The theory developed in the excerpt is based on statistics and scientific research to demonstrate that people's work discontentment finds an answer in the wrong former motivations of their first job choice. According to the author, the combination of personality traits and life interests would be the magic formula to find the ideal profession. The question is: Can magic really work in the real life?  Although this theory sounds like a promise of happiness in this world of complaints and disillusions, I think the most difficult part will be to find "the job" which matchs the best the two factors. I can't help but feel that despite its uplifting outcome, the solution offered by the article isn't realistically conceivable.%%

is gender equality still a hot topic in france? actually we have lot's of women in leading roles in business,art and politics, but it could be said these woman are over achieving and the majority of women do not share in this success. Nowadays,men still earned more than women for an equivalant job and qualification,during wold war two it must have seemed so different. After this period women obtained the right to vote, and in theoric they could have the same right than men, they are not only a simple wife who take care of her familly, but a real actor in the society.

My mother used to tell me that if both men and women could, each in his turn, get pregnant and if we supposed that women started, we wouldnt have more than three children by family. Take the time to think about it, isnt it true? In France gender equalities are registered in the law. Is it enough? The answerer is definitely NO. The main evidence is that many articles and TV report still devoted to this issue. They all point out that for the same job women still earn nearly 20 percent less than men. Even if the place of the women is no longer at home raising a family as her husband went out to work, she remains the one who puts her career enters bracket to rise children when she cant afford to pay a nanny. Then, it will be very difficult for her to find a new job or to come back to her previous position.

Since our every day life is a routine, we should try to make it less boring. For example, it sounds important to find a job which corresponds to your personality. Actually, we work 7 or 8 hours a day, which is a lot.  Unfortunately, not everybody has the choice or the ability to achieve their professional goals. If your job is thankless and unchallenging, you can try to consider it just as a means which allows you to earn money, to buy what you want, to travel, to have exciting hobbies. You can decide not to be defined by your work and not to let people do it.  I know, its easier to say than to do! However, its possible! The power of the mind is an amazing thing. However, what seems to be more difficult is to find the ultimate meaning of life. Why are we in the earth? What are we supposed to do? Maybe, everybody could find their own answer? ... Meanwhile, why not living every single day as if it were your last?

Dear Sir, I am writing regarding my request to be paid of the overtime I did. When you hired me, we have signed a contract that clearly states that I should be compensating for weekend work. Furthermore, my lawyer has noticed that nothing was written that allows you to refuse to pay me. As I am in my rights, I demand to be paid for two weekends rapidly. As a result, if you do not, I will have to sue you for damages.  Kind regards, VV

topic: Helping a friend find a job
text: I attended the meeting which the board of directors decided what people they had to fire and they needed help to make the right choice. After some different talks between the CEO, the COO and the CFO, I suggested them they delete my department because it was a mess and I was under the pressure all the time. They were very surprised by my proposal but all things considered the CEO agreed. It was a smart move for me and for the company. I didn't have any regrets even though it was a very difficult decision.

I am a computer engineer in a big insurance company. I like my work. Actually, it is intellectually interesting. For example, I talk with our users about their needs and I can give them some advices for a better product. I dont earn much money but it is enough for me because I live single and I have not to satisfy an exigent family. I have a little house in the country and a horse in the stables near my job. In summer I can bring my horse in a little field at home. Its great! Despite I spend a lot of money for my horse, I am getting by. My social life is very varied. I have colleagues, I have many friends from the horse environment and some old university friends, without forgetting my family! I like my life. I make different things, I meet persons very different with various characters and often passionated. There is a place for the unexpected, the surprise.

topic: Helping a friend find a job
text: At the beginning of the meeting, I presented two items of the agenda and also asked if somebody wanted to talk about of something else.  the main points were: -The goals of the company. -Strenghts and weaknesses face of the competition. Several persons were presented at the meeting: the CEO, the CFO, and the COO. Firstly, to introduce, the goals are to be more competitive face of the globalization and diversify our activities in order to sensitize other clients and obtain other market share. However, the CFO, CEO and COO agreed it but they asked me with which budget. So I told them that would be the next step. Secondly, we talked about the strenghts and weaknesses: the location of the company and the possibility to mege with another company in order to be more strong. Finally, The CFO didn't have time enough and told us to take couple of time for thinking about it and schedule a next meeting for developing our ideas.

Montpellier is very well know for its danse festival which takes place each year by the end of June. During 15 days, we can see on stage the biggest and nicest companies coming worldwide. What makes it unique is on one side the diversity of the performances but on an other side the diversity of the places where the shows occured. Indeed, it can be for example traditional dances from china, but also Hip-Hop or very modern dances. We can see the perfomances in the street, in the opera or even in outside theaters. It's really amazing how many things happen in only 15 days. It' just like a big fireworks at the end of the academic year. I personally enjoy modern dance and the beauty of bodies dancing on baroque music. I should recommend everyone to come to visit Montpellier in June to enjoy the sun, the beach and the festival.

In my country Cameroon, we have a lot of regions, and each region has its own culture. I'll talk about the west region and its traditional dance. When you see this traditional dance, it looks like anything but dance. The dancer wear a particular kind of clothes. These clothes has made with animal's skin like panther, lion or even crocodile. Every part of this dance is planned, the voice seems came from the core of the planet, and the music is very intense. They used a lot of drums, and it begins like the sound of your beating heart. Beside, you have a lot of people with very ugly mask who takes you to the center of the place and you have to dance with them. In addition, the environment is very hectic because there is also a very big smoke in this place. The dance is really spectacular, if you have to spend some time in my country never miss it.

topic: Helping a friend find a job
text: Hello, I send you this mail to summarize you today's meeting about the new organisation of the Research and Development Department. As you know, our company is growing up very quickly and we should come up with new products to stay competitive. The Randamp;D Department's Manager said that we should figure out a solution to program more industrials essays. I answered it would be difficult considering the production's charge of the factory even if I know it is necessary. We decided to implement a new organisation to optimize the Randamp;D's assays. Other meetings should be schedule soon. I would keep you informed about them. Valrie

text: At this meeting, our chief told us that the merger with Sunset will be likely to include some replacements of executives. Some of us have agreed, some others have disagreed. And our chief informed us that the too CEOs will be replaced and that a new post of chief operating executives will be created; and thereby that she turned down the offer made to her by the Board about this post.

In my country Cameroon, there is a lot of differences about gender. Females have many role: they have to clean the house, to provide all need of his husband, to educate the children. Men have to bring enough money for the family. They are the responsible of the family. Sometimes we can see changes about these role. In some couple we can have a woman who is working like the man, and they split all the houses task. In this case, the role of the man and the woman depends only on the agreement of each other.

topic: Applying to be a fitness trainer
text: I have recently assisted to a presentation dealing with the business of the main part of the world. I really have to say that the audience was calm and attentive thanks to the speaker's insurance and ability to express clearly his ideas. He looked at anyone and answered each question after a short reflexion. I think it is a good point. Moreover, he took the time to introduce the subject very well, expressed easily the main ideas and the key points and ended the presentation with a splendid conclusion. But I have to precise that this presentation wasn't perfect. I thought it a bit long and he could go faster on secondary points. We could have been more attentive if there were not so many pie charts! But, it rests an excellent show, which let us see the speaker's talents to speak clearly and strongly.

Dear Mr McDonald, I'm writing regarding an issue of breach of contract. As far as my claime for overtime payment is concerned, my contract clearly states that I should be compensated for weekend work. Furthermore, nothing in the contract allows the company to refuse to pay me for something that is not your fault. In this point, you are veru much taking the moral high ground. Consequently, TransWorld Entreprises should pay me for working the two weekends and I believe I am within my rights to object to being refused overtime pay. If I do not receive overtime pay, I intend to sue TransWorld Entreprises for damages. Your sincerely, Sophie

I am retired, so is my husband. We feel very lucky and happy.  At first it was a great pleasure to stop working. The problem wasn't that we didn't like our jobs, but things were turning to the worse: more and more administrative constraints. Now, we just do what we want. For instance learning English ! I love movies, and as Paris is a town with many theaters, I try to see as many films as possible. I can visit friends, attend workshop (a writng one), help my daughter in law and my son to look after my grandson. That is my favourite activity ! Travelling is not really our dream except, in my opinion, if we can meet really people, which means, for me, work with them as I did in the past. However, we decided to visit Brazil, a small part of Brazil. It was quite interesting but, in spite of our efforts to learn portuguese, it was not enough to get to know people. We have had another experience in the States. A satisfying one because we met American people in Paris, became friends, invited them and were invited in return. That's what I really enjoy. Eight year later, we are still in contact and try to meet in different places either in France or in the States.%%

topic: Finding a home for a wealthy client
text: I 've receive for my birthday a hour of lesson to drive a little airplane, a cessna. I says to my friends "Are you kidding me ? I have vertigo". But I don't wimped out me ! I love challenge. The day of the lesson, I was so scared that I misunderstand the adress. Finally when I arrived, the instructor was so kind, that I lost all my scary. I go up to the airplane, and i listen all the explanations. It was clear. The airplane had the double command. In 10 minutes, i was in the sky, driving then plan ! Oh my gosh ! I was the pilot ! It was so amazing. All around me was so little. Paris, Versailles, the eiffel tower ! But in sky i was so exciting that i haven't successfully begin the landing and we are going down a little bit rapidly. The instructor asked me to do an other tour, and he took the command for landing. I finished as passenger admiring the panorama.

In France, smoking in public areas is banned. Indeed, people are not allowed to smoke cigarettes in hospitals, airports, train stations, schools, workplaces and any public area that is closed or covered. Those who ignore the ban face a fine of 75 euros. However, some of the French smokers are interpretating the ban as an infringement of their freedom, rather than a public health measure. If you ask me, I agree with the law. I mean smoking is a dangerous habit that not only affects the smoker, but those around him or her. People who smoke subject themselves to deadly diseases by choice. Why should non-smokers be forced to be around it? They should not be subjected to the health risks that come from inhaling second-hand smoke. People's rights to breathe fresh and clean air should be protected, and along with that their right to maintain a healthy lifestyle. On the other hand, the ban is a good way to prevent teenagers from starting to smoke. Thus, preventing them from getting addicted to cigarettes.

Dear Teacher, I'm 45 years old and the technology has changed my life these last five years. Before, I was overwhelmed by the improvement of the technology. As an Engineer, I must be more productive and not engrossed by the evolution of the technology. My laptop and my smartphone with a wifi connexion have stimulated my day-to-day work. If I want to know something, an internet connexion provides me all the data I need. Internet is a revolutionary improvement for most of us who used to have the knowledge by reading books. My smartphone is also a marvelous product. I can phone all over my country. This smartphone can also be transformed to a GPS or a computer with an internet access... My HD TV is a significative evolution of the technology. This high quality of the screen and the possibility to have severals hundred of channels has changed my vision of the world. The objectivity of the media has been improved by this diversity of channels (instead of six channels before). It's also possible to watch a DVD at home by using your internet provider subscription without leaving your house. In my point of view, a touchpad seems to be useless and too much expensive for my need.%% Best regards Serge Blanc

I am satisfied on my life. I have a interresting job, even if it doesn't pay so much because of the crisis. But I don't live from paycheck to paycheck. I have fabulous children, they are curious and fascinated by the world. With them we visit museums,  do travel, cooking. I enjoy the life, go out with friends, receive them at home, go to cinema ... But sometimes I occasionally afraid about the futur. I rent my appartement, it's not mine. I have no possibility to buy an appartement. I can't buy everything i want and I have to make decision and choose spending. I am thinking about children study, how to pay them. The economic crisis makes the life more difficult. My chance is that i have a genial family, who helps each the other. My parents are fabulous grand parents. We know we will never be without roof, without eating. Globally I think, in actual context, life can't be perfect but it learns us to enjoy the importants things, family, healthy, friends.

One day, I was in a bus to go at school. The was empty so I take a sit because I don't like to stand up too long. At a moment an old woman get on the bus and put close to me even though there was two places near to us. She looked at me with a very insistent look. After some seconds, she was irritated and shouted "Young man, don't you see I was an old woman who needs to be sat ? If you were correctly educated, you'd leave your sit". I was so angry because there were two vacant places, but I leaved my sit. Two day after, I was again in the bus, and a new woman get on who seemed to be pregnant. To not appear as a bad young boy again, I decided to leave my sit and propose it to the woman, saying a woman who is pregnant should have a sit. She looked at me with a lot of rage, " I'm not pregnant". I was so embarrassed.

In my country, each year, the Festival d'Avignon presents between 35 and 40 different shows. It's one of the biggest Performing arts festivals in the world. It was in the "Cour d'honneur" of the Popes' Palace that festival d'Avignon began in 1947, directed by Jean Vilar, a famous engaged artist.This place was the main venue of the festival: an open-air theatre, for an audience of 2000 people, in the provencal night, at the heart of a monument on UNESCO's world heritage list. Since more than 60 years, during 4 weeks, from the morning to the night, the town offers theatre, dance, music,circus, videos of various styles from the most elitist to the most popular of the contemporary scene. At each corner of the old city you have an amazing choice of performances. A kind of fever takes to the streets because Avignon is also recognized for the "Off". How explain that about 1000 companies presenting works on their own initiatives, in about a hundred different venues. In the street, you can hear, all day long, these recurrent words: "what you saw"? "What you recommend me?" Last summer I liked this atmosphere...I believe it's unique and very interesting even if you aren't a specialist of theatre.

I am a doctor, a general practitioner. Prior this work, I studied medicine at the University in Lyon. I began to work thirty years ago in a little village, as partner with a colleague. I stayed there during two years before moving in another village. There, I worked alone during ten years. Afterwards, I move once more to work in another place with a new colleague. After eleven years together, we moved in the same village to a health center we had made built.I work there for seven years. I have the good luck that the economy doesn't affect my carrer. Regarding health, we don't know any bubble burst. These two last years, I worked only half time as doctor, because I manage a national association around health organization in primary care. I am the president of this association. For the future, I begin working about a new supply society for primary care teams in France. I guess it will start next year.

topic: Writing a report on staff satisfaction
text: I have been learning English for around 10 years. However, paradoxically I am still not completely fluent in English. I guess it mainly due to the French system which stress too much time on the grammatical's aspect rather than the oral and written expression. Due to the fact that English will be a large part in my future life I decided to study abroad two years ago. I went to Netherlands where the lessons were teached in English. On top of that, I am now using this website to further improve my level of English for my professional career but also because I really enjoyed talking in English. Indeed, I intend to work abroad at the end of my studies in Finance, in a place like London, Dubai or maybe New-York. The fact that I am also speaking french, I hope will be a advantage for me when I will trying to get the job of my dream.

To answer this question, I would say in the first place : yes, I do believe that important companies have a big role to play in their public image and in the general behavior of consumers. Such those companies are carrying an important image and they look like examples or even references to thousands of people. For instance, let's take Apple Inc.. Steve Jobs changed the life of millions of people by developing a new vision of computer softwares with Mac. And then people starting to think differently about the Internet and new technologies. They now see it as a place where everything must be simple to use and intuitive. But Apple has also an image to keep : they must avoid such scandals as those with the Foxconn company, which is in charge of iPhone building. To finish, I would say that big companies have an important part to play. They must carry a good image of businness, and not the idea that their belong only to their shareolders. For instance, in france, the government is encouraging people to buy French products and not only products which come from abroad. CSR is a matter for all of us.

Montpellier is situated, in the South of France a few miles away from the Mediterranean coast. But the tides are not very strong, so the tidal energy is not the right renewable power to choose for this place. Alternatively, the region of Montpellier is crossed by a stong river, called "Le Lez", that might be a great potential for the local population, as well as for the environment. So, a dam could be built accross The Lez. And since Montpellier population is increasing steadily , this investment would not cost a lot, because 450000 inhabitants can easily raise $75000 project costs, as part of their local taxes. In addition, that there would be no fuel costs for this project, and the maintenance cost will be very low. Consequently, Montpellier population would use a renewable energy, for a lower cost than the energy they are using now. If I were the mayor of Montpellier, I would do my best to save my city from pollution, and thus preserve the environment. In my opinion, using hydroelectric energy in Montpellier would be a big step towards building an environmentally friendly society. It seems as simple as that, but politicians don't really practice what preach.%%

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: Needs and goals: Phisiological I think most of my physiological needs are being met.I consider myself very lucky given that so many people in the world are strugling to meet their basic needs and live under the poverty line. Safety I live in a safe environment. I have some worries regarding my financial situations and how I am gonna pay my tuitions fees for the rest of my scolarity; but I'm confident I'll sort this out. Belongness and love I benefit from a lot of support from my family which helps me a lot. I intend to get married as soon as I get my degree in financial engeenering an find a good job. I find that having a partner in your life is very important. It completes you and give you the strenght and motivation to strive and do well in your life. Esteem I want to overcome my shyness and be more confident and assertive. Self-actualization: I want to get a better understanding of human being's psychology so that I'll be more empathetic and sympathetic.%%

Success is often associated with financial and social success. The image that comes to my mind when I think of success is a hard-working and busy businessman, who is very rich and respected by people. I imagine a man who runs behind schedule but who has the time to show his wonderful and elegant woman during fashionable evenings. Businessmen are respected because they embody the power. On the contrary, a spiritual person like Soeur Emmanuelle, who owned nothing, was admirable because she was herself and made us aware of the essential things. I consider that Soeur Emmanuelle succeeded her personal life because she remained faithful to herself and to her deepest aspirations. She found the meaning of her life by believing in God and by helping the most needy. However, you can find some similarities between these two examples. The businessman and the spiritual person both have a goal and do their best to reach it. They both have qualities and skills that lead them to success like intelligence, a great involvement in what they do, an extraordinary strength of character and perseverance. Despite these similarities, I cant help thinking that the businessman is just gifted like thousands of businessmen whereas Soeur Emmanuelle is an exceptional human being.%%

Every day you can observe that companies are more worried about competitiveness than about respect for their employees or environment. Lately, there was a big fire in Bangladesh in which quite a lot of European and American firms were involved. How many workers, mostly women, died in this accident? How many CEO felt directly or indirectly responsible?  Dont you remember, too, the oil spills in Brittany, Alaska and Gulf of Mexico? The only law seems to be competitiveness. Market shares, shareholders dictate their rules and corporate social responsibility seems to be no more than a wishful thinking. Reality being what it is, would it be responsible to give up? There are so many powerful and gigantic international groups dominating not only economy and finance but also news and our minds that the temptation is to get desperate. Are we allowed to give up? Did Muhamad Yunus, Nobel Peace Prize, founder of the Grameen Bank give up?  In one of the poorest countries in the world, he invented new economical and successful concepts. In our own country, France, the social economy won today a true victory :Les Atelires who were supposed to be defeated, two days ago, were given enough money, from private donators, to save their jobs.%% What a symbol to celebrate the day of the women tomorrow!

One month ago, a terror attack has been place in Russia in the city of Volgograd. The death toll rose to 16 people dead. Just before the starting of the winter Olympic Games in Sotchi this week, there has been mounting critics about the organization about this controversial Olympic Games. First of all, there are a lot of critics about the cost of these games, around 50 billions dollars, which is five times larger than the organization of the Vancouver Olympic Games. Besides some experts believe that there has been a lot of corruption in the different contracts deliver by the president Poutine. Second of all, some westerns countries are worried about the treatment of gay people in Russia. Indeed, a new bill has passed recently, and gay people are not allowed to kiss each other on public squares. That is why presidents such as Barack Obama or the French president ,Francois Hollande would not attend the inaugural ceremony.

Green business is important to me. To be truly green, a business should consider using renewable energy, such as solar or wind power, or an office with a green roof. Businesses that sell green products have to conserve natural resources as much as possible by using organic, recycled and biodegradable materials in their products and packaging. I don't go out of my way to buy green products, but I will pay a lot more for certain items. It's not always easy to find truly green products, and I don't want to use gasoline by driving to buy green products. However, I am concerned about the food I eat, so I sacrifice other luxuries in my life to make sure I'm eating healthy food. Of course, you have to take a company at its word that the product is truly green, which is why I buy from companies I know a lot about.

During the latest 30 years of the XXth. century, many famous landmarks were built in Paris: Centre Georges Pompidou, Montparnasse Tower, Louvre Pyramid, and Bastille Opera. Some of them were very controversial. But one thing is certain, they all will left a long lasting footprint in the urban landscape. In my opinion, the Centre Georges Pompidou is the most obvious failure. Its groundbreaking architecture and its garish aggressive colors are unnecessarily provocative and upsetting. In opposite, the Louvre Pyramid is certainly an unquestionable achievement. This magnificent glass and steel structure in the center courtyard stands out against the ancient architecture of the Louvre palace. It has become the main entrance of the place. Each visitor can experience how this museum is modern and living and no more as asleep as it was before Pyramid's construction. To my taste, it's a masterpiece. Unfortunately, nothing that deserves to be mentioned has been built for ten years. The architectural policy is static since the mid 90's The Seine banks have been refurbished and cleared of cars. An important part of them are now a pedestrian area. But that's an improvement, not an ambitious urban policy. Without doubt, Paris needs a challenging architectural vision to be able to compete with other world renowned metropolises.%%

Well, I gave a look at the list of propositions we had from some companies to choose a kind of renewable energy for our city. I choose wind power. The reason why I choosed that particular kind of energy is for several reasons : First, we here live by the sea, and the city is build alongside the shores, and we have wind all days. Second, the impact of the installation on the environment is quite low, so we could think about put turbines in many different places without have the fear of destructing the ecosystem if we do that. But I know, the cost is non-negligible (80000$), but I think if we do this, in less than ten years we should be able to reduce our cost of electricity by twice and save up more money to build new ecological facilities in town. It's a bet, but I'm sure everybody here is willing to do this if this is the better way we find to reduce the footprint of our city and habitants.

It seems to me that successful people have many points in common. First, they live their lives in a positive way. They never stand too long on negative points and always think about what should be the next step to succeed. Whatever happens to them, they are ready to bounce back. In addition to that, they are persistant, passionate and ready to adapt themselves (or their ideas) to the market's needs. Of course, they also have a strong need to be their own boss and need to do things in their own way. However, sometimes, you can meet people who have successful people's traits and habits but are considered by other people as "loosers". Yet, when you talk to them, you are surprised to see they still believe in themselves, feeling that one day their turn will come up and they will be successful. To do so, they will need the only thing you have to get to be successful : the idea ! Without it, they will continue to be a successful person only in their own fantasy world.

I live in the country where no buldings have been built. Only small houses and farms... But my village is not so far from a big city : Lyon in France. Lyon is the second city in France after Paris. A quarter is famous in Lyon : the Part-Dieu. It is famous because it is considered as the second business place in France. There is a lot of buildings and a few skyscrapers. The best wellknowed was called the Credit Lyonnais tour, then the "Tour de la Part Dieu" . The habitants call it : the pencil, because top is a pyramid. It is a red and circular building. It is 165 meter high. The seven highest floors are occupied by an hotel. The second skyscraper in this city is the tour Oxygen. I like the architecture of this other building. It is only 115 meter high, but 80% of the surface is made with glass. It is really beautiful. Even if I prefer the country...

I think my town is a masterpiece of the 14th century's architecture. In fact, I live in Avignon, France, where you can found some amazing buildings. First, the Pope's Palace, a huge castle inside the town, where popes used to live in the XIVth century. It is a very touristic  and a wonderful place that you can visit. On the top of the palace, there is a gold statue of virgin Marie, which shows the story of my town and which is particularly beautiful. The front of the monument is impressive and the park just above the palace, on a hill, is as wonderful. It's a relaxing place where I love to go with my friends or family. In Avignon, there are also a lot of small streets, really old and welcoming and some other parks with trees and benches. The library is an old church which has been renovated so we can feel secure and relax there. In fact, you don't need to love ancient architecture to love Avignon, because you can't think this town's architecture is not great, that's why a lot of people come here quite often!

At the beginning, Paris (capitale of France) was a small village. Since the middle age, most French kings wanted to make their mark on this town which has never been destroyed. Paris houses then an impressive number of historical and famous monuments which are worth visiting. What also characterises Paris are rows of buildings along tree-lined avenues.Those five-story buildings are well known for their maintained facades and their balconies on the fifth floor. In the 1830's, Rambuteau conceived this architecture with wide boulevards and large avenues in order to have the main areas cleared and to make public transport easier. Afterwards, the plan was developed by Haussmann. In addition to those typical neighbourhoods of Paris, no building better symbolises the capital of France than the Eiffel Tower. Indeed, you couldn't possibly visit Paris without seeing the Eiffel Tower which was built for the 1889 International Exhibition. The tower which was the highest structure in the world from 1889 until 1930 rises 300 meters. You can see its top from all over Paris.

A place you should have to visit!%%

The buildings of Shanghai are simply breathtaking in their size and diversity. The city has an impressive number of modern skyscrapers  more than a thousand, according to some counts  that are in sharp contrast to several styles of architecture from different time periods. The Bund offers the perfect tour of sophisticated European buildings, many constructed in the mid-1800s to early 1900s in various gorgeous styles, including Renaissance, Baroque and neo-classical. The area is attractively situated along the Huangpu River. Look across the Huangpu for one of the city's most intriguing architectural contrasts: Pudong, with buildings that look like they belong in a science-fiction movie. Here, you'll immediately notice Shanghai's most famous building, the Oriental Pearl Tower. It's definitely not the world's most beautiful building, but it is one of the tallest (at 468 meters) and most unusual. The spire and antennas reach an impressive 118 meters high. Near the Pearl is the Shanghai World Financial Center, which, with 101 floors, is China's tallest building.

text: When i see my high school friend, we often discuss about our differents schools or universities which we have choose after the high school. In contrary, in preparatory class, you are followed by your teacher and you are not allowed to miss one hour without justification. The number of student is different to, in university, lectures  can take place with one undred students, whereas in preparatory class you are by group of 30. In one hand, there are petty crimes, purse snatching and shoplifting, often occurred by young people, those lead to juvenile detention. In the other hand there are violent crimes: hardened criminals, murderer, terrorist which can not be considered the same way and often lead to incarceration. The juvenile detention gives higher chance to young people for rehabilitation than incarceration, provided that young people can get a new or different education in these special centers. The main goal of incarceration is to protect society from violent criminals activities and second to prepare criminals to their rehabilitation. But the chances of success are smaller as violent criminals are not committed to renew their education, consequently hardened criminals might be considered beyond hope. As far as I am concerned, crime could be considered differently in my country: even if the differentiation between petty crimes and violent crimes is clearly made in the court, but unless there is a lack of juvenile detention centers, young people can be incarcerate as well as hardened criminals. The consequences are disaster as recidivism gets higher and juvenile criminals get hardened.

France, is a country well-known for it's wonderful and mostly old-fashioned architecture. The towns of France make you travel to another time. topic: Writing about a disaster relief effort
text: We will operate the disaster recovery plan mainly in three steps. First of all, we will restore all the data from the backup site. Secondly, well start all the applications and finally we will ask the users to perform all the tests to make sure that the data restored are consistent.  The data restoration is a process that will take twelve hours since there are four servers to be restored and the average restoration time per server is three hours.  The starting of all applications, we'll take at least one hour and a half. This duration also takes into account the tuning of applications components and the technical tests to make sure that all parts are working properly and interacting with each other.  The users tests will include authentication, basic and complex requests execution and then results analysis.  The success of such an exercise will depend on how well we will prepare it and our capacity to quickly handle any problem not foreseen.

I live in Paris. In this city, there is no very high building but a lot of unusual architecture. The most famous is certainly the Eiffel Tower, symbol of Paris in the world. This tower was constructed by Gustave Eiffel for the Universal Exposition in 1889, in order to symbolize the capacity of the french industry. What's funny is that at the beginning, this monument was not loved by the French and Parisians, and it should have dismantle just after the exposition. During its life, the Eiffel tower was painting in several colors, nowadays it is brown. The Eiffel Tower measure 324 meters and is composed by 3 floors. The first two can be visited by the tourists, with a restaurant in the first floor. On the other hand, the third floor is only use for antennas in order to diffuse TV and radio over the town.

Small start-up rise everywhere in the world and succeed on markets that big companies would like to lead.  But the behavior of these small start up is often to be bought and soon or later to be integrated. These acquisitions give social responsibilities to big companies regards to the process of people integration. Although the skill of integrated people is high and lead the start-up to be at the highest level on the market, it's easy to understand that some practical issues need to be solved: twice support services, twice CEO, twice IT manager etc.... As a consequence, this integration leads to a rapid reduction of human resources and gives to the buyer responsibilities of people selection and replacement. But what would be the final image of the company after this process? The buyer may help or at least gives a free pass to people in their way to find an alternative activity inside or outside the company. The buyer should consider past and future of excluded people . One important reason is not to create your future worst competitor . Another important reason is to prevent bad advertising that degrades the commercial image of owned brands. Give respect to people that are no longer necessary in the organization is surely a corporate social responsibility with payback.%%

Green business differ from standard business in that it offers to customers a product with more respect to environmental concerns and it gives a kind of guaranty that you do not support a disturbing activity. I will choose vegatables of a local producer at the expense of an imported product, provided that the local producer is environmentally friendly. Naturally, local businesses are reducing transport and so the carbon footprint unlike imported products. As the local producer is most probably living in the same area as I do, I have just to go out of my way a little bit to buy his product. In additition, we should consider that tons of vegatables are imported from foreign countries, while arable land are neglected or abandoned, and while people are desperately unemployed. Local business can be considered as natural Green business, in that CO2 footprint is reduced and employment is preserved, to the extent that it is respectfull to environmental concerns.

CSR is a divisive issue. Some people denounce the hypocrisy and the cynicism of corporate social responsibility the sole aim of which is to make money. Indeed, numerous surveys have emphasized that consumers tend to support companies which are involved in a social cause. Although those activities dont provide an immediate financial benefit to the companies, we know how much positively they affect their image. It is often said that it is just a marketing device. Of course, its true but should we expect the companies to be philanthropist? In so far as its a win-win situation, why should we blame CSR? Let me give you examples of Kellogg which plans to provide more than half a billion breakfasts by 2016 or of Sparkly Cola which is going to help underprivileged high school students by donating $200,000 in scholarships. Whether its a PR trick or not, the fact is it really helps needy people. Although companies are free to choose their own policy, they have duties toward their employees who must be respected and fairly paid. Besides, companies which pollute the environment have to clean it up.

If you visit the south of France, it will be worth to have a stop at Montpellier. You will be intrigued by the architectural contrast that has been elaborated since the city's establishment, in the 10th century. The historical city center, built in early middle age, is a real labyrinth. You can be lost in the narrow streets which tell you the story of the city, through their beige stone facade houses, that nestle a number of well-maintained private properties, dating back to the 10th Century. You can also find 19th-century Haussmannian style buildings, standing around the main city square "place de la comdie". The most impressive building at the Comedy square is the theater, its facade is exactly similar to the one at Opera Garnier in Paris. Antigone is the modern part of Montpellier. Built in the 1990's, it confirmed the architectural contrast of the city. The most dominant building in that area is "Languedoc Roussillon region's hall", a daring glass Arch, standing on the Lez riverside, it is 50 meter high, the tallest building in the city center. In Montpellier you can have the opportunity to go through three different eras in less than a day!%%

I think the question of CSR is very simple and it's suprising that it could be a divising issue. On the one hand, companies are taking all non sustainable ressources on earth for obtaining big profits. What more natural they pay back for that, in giving a better life to society? On the other hand, when companies argue they have nothing to do with society's problems because it's it'the government's job, they are not logical with themselves. If we sould follow their rules, governement would be without public interest! The market is only the one to rule everything. No need goverment and all its regulation! I think all these speeches about morality in companies are funny and really naive. Howether, I recognize CSR is pragmatic, like all the anglo-saxon culture: "A well paid worker is a good worker". Good! Good for them! Good for the company! But I think we forget the real meaning of life, the respect of the planet we have to give back to our children. Not a question about companies but a fundamental question about real human rigths.

I'm a firm believer in sustainable living. I believe that instead of relying on fossil fuels, we should make more use of renewable energy, that is wind and water power, the energy of the sun. Provided that you have strong winds,wind power is quite effective. Building a small wind farm will provide our whole village with electricity. It's a cheaper way of generating energy. Why don't we built our houses with traditional energy-saving techniques. The way I look at it, we can do more. If each individual does their part, we can move mountains! In my opinion, we have to be active ourselves

A lot of businesses exist simply to make money. There are others who seriously which to provide a needed service to a community or to the world. Each of these businesses has a corporate responsibility to the public, its shareholders and the world it trades in. Years ago, a company corporate responsibility was dictated by its government. Today, however, corporate responsibility has to take into account the world that we live in on a much wider scale. The public has become much more globally aware, and there are a number of groups that monitor corporations closely. These groups have the conditions of the world in mind. They think about the social issues of the world, such as labor laws and the exploitation of workers. They are also concerned with environmental issues, such as the rainforests disappearing. Large global companies know that people are watching them and that any wrongdoing will not go unnoticed. Personnally, I think companies must have a social conscience, treat employees fairly and try to do the best for their shareholders while trying to be socially responsible. Corporate responsibility has a huge impact on the local community, but also on the world. Bad and good corporate responsibility has effects that reach from the worker in the third world country to the air that we breathe.%%

There are definitely both pros and cons associated with superstores, but I think we have to be realistic about the circumstances of some communities. I understand the argument that superstores create primarily low-wage jobs and provide few if any social or health benefits to those same workers. I also understand that these giant stores could possibly bankrupt smaller stores that are unable to compete with them. I think just about everyone knows this to be true. On the other hand, if a community is already in economic trouble, with few jobs available and people struggling, saying 'no' to a superstore doesn't seem to make any sense at all. Isn't a low-wage job better than no job at all? And, if a community is struggling, wouldn't the lower prices that a superstore can offer help those most in need? I want to reiterate that I understand that superstores are not the answer for every community. Different communities have different circumstances and different needs. All I'm saying is that, under certain circumstances, a superstore might do more good than harm for a struggling community.

I would like to talk about the nicest buildings you can find in France. I personaly like very much the city of Paris. That's amazing how many interresting buildings we can find here. There are not so impressing by  their respective height but rather to their styles. By going to the center of Paris, where are located the biggest and well-known shopping centers of " Printemps" , " les Galeries Lafayette", you will see here what we call "haussmanienne architecture" . It's a specific style. I do not know with accuracy when it was created but I guess it was in the 1800's century. Now you may want to see more modern buildings. All biggest french and foreign headquarters of business companies are located on a small place as built on a total suface of 25 square kilometers, not more. They are not so impressive by their height compared to the ones we can find in Schanghai for instance. But they are all ultra-modern and regrouped arond the " Arche de la Defense" . This realization has been built by the old French president Franois Mitterand before ending his political carreer.%%

It's not really easy to pinpoint success since everyone can succeed in some things in his life and fail in some other ones. Most of the successful persons we usually admire have been successful in their public life but they did not always met the same success in their private life. as for example, Mahatma Gandhi or Martin Luther King Jr are considered as two of the best leaders of the XXth century ; they have changed the world by showing how to live in peace all together and by helping people to become better persons. However, most of their biographers consider that in their private life they both were far to be perfect, caring or faithful husbands. Similarly, so many popular actors, singers, politicians or businessmen have failed or never succeeded to be truly happy in their private life. Marilyn Monroe, the most beautiful woman in the world committed suicide and Howard Hughes, one of the main shareholders of TWA, spent the last years of his life alone suffering from mental problems... The line between success and failure is often really thin.

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: I'm European, more precisely French. European Union existed before I was born so I've known it my whole life. For example, I know that I can travel to United Kingdom, Spain, Germay and almost each European country freely, i.e. I don't need Visa, Embassy authorization or anything else, I am free to move to each of these country just because I want, like any another place in France. As a consequence, I get used with this idea that in addition of the fact to be French, I am European, and Europe is, to an extent, my home. We have a unique currency with an important number of countries, which is something enjoyable to travel. I mean, I use to go to Spain for my summer holidays, and I know that I don't need a particular ID paper, change currencies before to travel or anything else. Moreover, since this summer, fines received with our cars in Spain and Germany cross the border too, and have to be paid to the Spanish and German governments.

topic: Writing about a disaster relief effort
text: Dear all, I am writing to you to keep you informed about the Diamond project. I have a very good news to give you, the software "Diamond" is finally installed and ready to use. I would like to thank you all for your commitment. First of all, we have to schedule an agenda for the training on the software as soon as possible. Furthermore, we have to book a special room which everyone could look at the projector and make suggestions. I propose to gather some special group belonging in each department for optimizing the time-training. In addition, we have to collect some information to enter the data in the computer as example. I will organize a brainstorming soon, as all ideas are welcome! Please let me know your availabilities. If you have any questions or concerns regarding this project, please let me know. Best regards.

Located on the equator, Dienga is a small city of about 1200 inhabitants. The city council has decided to invest into renewable energy source for its needs in electricity. This decision had been motivated by three main reasons.  First of all, due to its distance from the power grid, it would be costly in terms of time and money to extend power lines to Dienga. Secondly, because of its geographical position, there is a lot of sun over the city almost all year around. The installation of Solar panels takes only a couple of weeks and requires the services of two specialists whose responsibility is to get the proper panels and install them on all people's roofs. Finally, the whole project that amount up to $120 000 will be financed by the local community whose main activity is sugar cane production. For Dienga's people, Solar power is not only environmentally friendly but also is the most cost-effective source of energy.

There is a big number of big-box stores which have been created since a few decades. For instance, on my island, namely Corsica, there are four big-box stores just for Bastia, a small city on the North of the Island. I think such stores have both positive and negative effects on communities. Concerning the pros, such stores can provide a wide range of products at a price more interesting than those proposed by a little supermarket. Therefore people can afford more things and have more choice. They can buy a lot of things more quickly. Nowadays, the big-box stores also propose the delivery of the products directly at home for free. So, it's really convenient when you are busy. However, at the same time, big-box stores shows cons. First of all, local family-owned businesses often go to bust and go bankrupt because they can't keep up with the competition of the big-box stores. Their prices are too attractive. Secondly, the workers of the big stores get very low wages and they don't benefit from social and health advantages. Nevertheless with the economic crisis I keep thinking that it's always a good thing to have a big-box store around the corner.%% I would like to point out that it allows community to create new jobs and revitalize the economy even though such model of store presents some drawbacks previously mentioned.

As far as I remember, I have always lived in cities in which at least one big-box store was located. It is not really a recent evolution of the commercial industry, but it is true that they likely led to the decrease in the number of mom-and-pop shops in the dowtowns. Obviously, people are now used to purchasing their food and cosmetic in the shops of these big retailers, because these companies have succeeded to cut down on the prices when they negociate a large amount of products, with their suppliers. Furthermore, all you need is at the same place and you do not face any problems to park your car for example. To sum up, you gain time and money. However, there are not only pros when a new big-box store sets up in a commercial area. Generally, the market share of the mom-and-pop shops decrease because the craftmen usually apply less attractive prices than the biggest food retailers. In consequence, the social life in the downtown grows poorer, even if some small shops like backeries or drugstores continue to exist. Moreover, the quality of products in the big-box stores is never as good as the one in the craftmen's shops.%% Indeed, you can sometimes hear about food which was not appropriate to the consumption in the big-box stores, because they pushed the prices so lower that the quality would not follow.

topic: Writing about a disaster relief effort
text: Hello fellow colleague, As you know I have been working on a pretty big project for the past 6 months, and it is finally reaching to an end ! I was hopping you could come to my office during the week so that we could discuss about it and maybe you could quickly read my report to make sure I made an excellent work. Apart from that point I wanted to invite you to my birthday party which will take place in two weeks in my house. Plenty of nice people will be there and I guess you too. Have a good day and see you soon !

I would like to speak about the twins tower. I had visited them a couple of years and I can perfectly remember how they were so big, urge and of course high. In the maintain entrance you could find a special garden inside the skyscraper. It was very lightened place and when you entered inside you could feel the space with a lot of glasses. It was amazing to feel that in buildings. Even if I visited a lot of Paris and Geneva for many times, I don't think I will be able to find that same feeling. I felt something special. Not only the garden inside but this skyscraper was incredible. I tried to take a picture to the top and the bottom of this building but my camera wasn't be able to do it. Too hight!! Anyway I love so much New York, the atmosphere is really special and all this building higher make you feel as an ant. When I saw the tragedy of those towers on TV, I thought that it couldn't be possible! But believe me, imagine how urge is the plane! How urge were they! I keep at home a clock broken on my wall representing the twin towers!%%

Dear Elizabeth, Thanks a lot for these three images and poposition for our campaign. You have made a good job. Since the begening of the brand, we use the image of the sportive watch and we focus more on the man consumer and it work really well! The public consider our watches as casual watches. But today, for this new collection, i want more ! I want to surprise the consmer and i believe we should change our trading policy. I have decided that our new selling key is the smart watch which you must wear with the smoking or the luxury jacket for the reception. And i believe we must focus more on the women who wants some beautiful watches to wear with their dress and jewelry. And for this great changement i want use poesy and beauty. To that, i have chosen the first image an slogan :" Century is a timeless beauty".

In my opinion green business is definitely one new way of doing business. That's a new type of business that impacts us in our daily life. It is both a new tool from marketing point of view and an intelligent way how to preserve all natural ressources. But I personaly consider that people are too much manipulated. When watching TV, we are given so many information regarding new products, new brands that, in the end, we are almost obliged to buy some of those green products. Some people even if they can't afford buying them will sacrifice some other things in their life in order to be a member of I call " the green community". Marketing concept has created new needs to people. On the other hand I need to admit that, yes, natural ressources are geeting scarce. It's a just a fact we can not ignore it at all. We all know that the worlwide population is drastically increasing. The earth will have to do more to feed that population but we all know that our planete is already under capacity. In that sense, the green industry is doing its part by providing some solutions such as recycling old products, reducing any kind of waste.%% One good example is the recycling of old clothes into new materials to create new products.

I worked at Honeywell Technologies for several years. I learned a lot from that company. For instance I'd like to point out the fact that Honeywell is deeply involved in CSR. Along the years Honeywell demonstrated in many cases that it contributes to help poor people, support education programs of young students or also is able to create a specific fundation in charge to help people who have been impacted by severe climatic conditions such as hurricane, tsunami, earthquakes....I realized that Honeywell is a well-known company by its products and its quality of service but also because it is considered as a company costantly engaged in many volonteer works programs. For sure it is an excellent way to developp this company's image and keep in close touch with a diversified community all over the world. To give a clear and significant example of what Honeywell is able to do, its board decided several years ago to offer possibility to children of its employees to join Honeywell's academy. That program dedicated to young students from the age of 16 to 19, allows them to discover new scientific or engineering programs. We can think it is a win-win situation as those students with their excellent technical background will have later possibility to apply to some vacant positions at Honeywell.%% Serving local community is also a way how to better communicate on the company and creating futur talents of Honeywell.

topic: Writing about a disaster relief effort
text: Mr. Smith, I'm writing to update you on our progress. I'm sure you will be glad to know that we have tested the samples and determined that the quality was excellent. Although, we identified an issue with some of the packaging materials, we immediately fixed it. Then, the marketing department sent the samples to sever key partners for further testing. Once we receive the feedback, we will develop several marketing campaigns for your review. We plan to mail the materials to you on July. Alternatively, you could visit our headquarters after July to discuss them. In either case, it's critical that we receive your comments by July 22. I can arrange a meeting to touch base the week of August 8th. Could you let me know whether that works for you ? Best regards, T. Bobbeline

I believe companies have a moral responsibility to give back to society. Let me give you an exemple. Sparkly Cola Soft Drink Co. announced today that it will partner with local schools across the state to provide scholarships for underprivileged high school students to attend college. The Sparkly Cola Scholarship Program will award 20 scholarships in the amount of $10,000 each, starting next year. Scholarships will be determined by local school boards, based on need and academic achievement. The people of New York have made Sparkly Cola one of their favorite soft drinks, and they just felt like it was time for them to give back to communities across the great state of New York," Sparkly CEO Sandy Parks said. "Our donation of $200,000 is a lot of money, but nothing is too good for our kids. And we hope to do even more in the future."

text: Dear Ms Demaris, I am writing to inform you about our advancement in the new business game. We have finished the third round and are waiting for the rules concerning the round 4. The team want to add that we really appreciated this business game and looking forward to the next round. However, we have one question we intend to ask you. It is related with the grade on this evaluation. We want to know if we need to give you back a report at the end of the game or the grade we'll be based only on our ranking at the end of the game. Kind regards, Elanzize Abdallah (Group B)

text: European Union is an association of the european countries rules by the Treaty of European Union. Thes countries were, after the second world conflict, tired of all these wars which take place in this part of the world. Decide to unite to prevent conflicts and make a political and economical alliance. At the begening, there was only six countries, and they have mainly an economic goal with the Economic European Comunitie ( treaty of Roma in 1957) but since the treaty of Maastrich the organization was called European Union and have political goals to.  Now there are twenty eight countries. Which have a comon currency (Euro), except few countries as England who decide to conserve the livre. They are a European Parliament with a flag and an hymn and there is free trade between the countries. European Union is also a way to fight in the world economy, togethers, they are stronger.

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: I am living in the eurozone and I must say, I consider it ,as a good thing. Living at the german border, we have now the same currency, its easy to compare the prices of the goods. In the 90 s when I went to go shopping in Germany, I had to go to the bank and change money, now we have the same. we can travel easier, we have no more frontiers; More and more the two countries are working together like the police for example. Both states are comparing their livestyles and they are coming closer. Its also easier to study in other European countries. They sign a agreement, and can promote free trade, no more customs. Making deals inside of Europe is very simple and brings also people together.The members of Europe belive that theirs closer economic cooperation will make them stronger face to the rest of the world.

Dear Ms Hamilton, I looked carefully into the three images and slogans and my final choice is the image picturing two golfers with the slogan: Golf and Century.The perfect team. It was not an easy decision but let's me explain it. Century watches is a luxury brand, and what's better than golf to demonstrate your premium image. Indeed,the fact to be associated to a sport like golf could bring to the company potential customers and raise awareness to the public. Concerning the ad campaign we have foreseen to carry out several tv spot, radio spot and also advertisement in magazines which target customer are men with means over 35 years old. We intent to target all the different ads to your target customer in order to be the most efficient possible. If you have any questions or remarks, feel free to ask. I look forward to hearing from you regarding our decision. Kind regards. Elanzize Abdallah

The city of Lyon, formely known as Lugdunum during the ancient rome houses a lot of famous buildings or square to visit. The most famous square is the Place Bellecour which is the largest clear square of Europe with around 62 000 square meters. In the middle of the "Place Bellecour" there is a famous sculpture which is a horse mounted by the king Louis XIV. With regards to buildings, the tallest one is the" Tour du Credit Lyonnais" with rised to 165 meters and has 42 floors. The building is known for his shape of a pencil. In France, this building is the 10th highest skycrapers, the nine other tallest skycrapers or all located in Paris. I cannot finish this article without talking about the Mettalic tower of Fourviere which was built between 1892 and 1894 and has a striking ressemblance with the Eiffel Tower. Nowadays the tower serves only as a television relay tower.

CSR is a difficult question to deal with when you are running a business. For a long time, it was considered as a PR trick: companies gave back money to charities in order to have a good image on their consumers, it was also called "greenwashing" especially for companies who's businesses were involved in environment issues. The best example to me on that point is Total : an oil industry which causes a lot of damage all around the world because of their activity, such as the boat full of oil which broke up among the britain's coast, causing thousands of animals' deaths and a big impact on the local environment. It's now a part of the past: according to me, big companies have no choice now to take their responsabilities: new jobs were created in order to promote their actions and try to improve benefits among this issues.

Dear Elizabeth, After a thourough study we have selected the following ad campaign for Century watches. I'm pretty sure you will love it. First of all many people match Century watches with a certain concept of luxury. That's the reason why we tend to choose the picture representing a man playing to Golf, cause as you know Golf used to be considered as a gentlemen sport. But we would like to enlarge our public target by reaching out also women. In this way we have had an idea that will capture your attention : we wish to have, on the same ad, a professional woman Golf player associated to a man celebrity representing a lot of class, like George Clooney for instance. With the two of them, we could reach out a really large public, from almost all men catagories to an important number of women, doing sports or having a glamour side. Please let me know if this idea has fully caught your attention. Kind regards

In my country petty crimes don't send people in jail. If you are caught by police for shoplifting for instance you will just receive a blame, but if you do it several times you will be presented to a judge who will decide whether or not you have to go in prison. However, there is almost no chance that you go in jail. For medium crimes like purse snatching, mug or any kind of such crimes, you will be presented to a judge and probably condamned but due to the fact that prisons are full, you will certainly have to wear an electronic tag. For big crimes, like rape or murder, there will be no escape for you, you will go in jail. If you are just a suspect and before your trial, you will go in custody till the beginning of your process, because you are considered as dangerous for the society.

We don't have an ocean or a strong river in my area, so it will not be possible to use hydroelectricity or tidal energy, even biomass can't be conceivable. Here, some homes have panels solar on their roofs and I have to say that this installation is really costly.  For promoting them to the future owners, it was said that they will save money if they purchase it. The first assumption of this operation was to obtain a tax reduction and the second was to sell the electricity provided by this concept to the government. In fact, the reality is that this investment is too costly and the return on investment is not profitable, because the government of course, dropped the cost of electricity in return. Concerning the wind power I heard that it is not safe for the health, independently the fact of the nuisance of the loudly sound. I think that everyone should take care of our environment by recyclable each thing possible, and try to save energy each time we can do it, because the future does not appear as happy days nowadays.%%

I am living in Marseille which is one of the oldest city in FRANCE. In Marseille we do not have skycraper, but small old building, most of them have less than 5 flours. The city is a succession of small  villages whirh their own characters The tallest building in MARSEILLE is situated by the see, it is the CMA tower owned by the bigest french maritime company. This building is not built to be used for living, it's a business place. More than 2500 emplyees work at the CMa tower. Since the last 3 years a new district is Under construction and will replace ancient factories and ware houses located around the commercial Harbour. Tall building emerge but nothing to do with Shanghai. Even if the city is cosmopolite and open to various culture it remains a regional metropole Economic turn down and unemployment limit the development of business and commercial centers.

In my country, the maxmum punishment for a criminal is to be sentenced for life empriosonment. All criminals even those who have done petty crimes have to be judged. Because of the increasibng numbers of crimes, Poeple involved have sometimes to wait for months before being judged. Another major issue, is life conditions in jaIL. Actually the number of places available in jail is not sufficient in comparison with the number of criminals supposed to be emprisonned. The result is that prisons are overloaded. Where prinsoners should be 2 per rooms, they have to share their cell with 3 or 4 others guys. this creates por conditons of Hygiene and difficulties for the staff working for security. A report was recently sent to rthe governnement in order to force it to take appropriate measure to ensure that kife conditions in jail are respecting human ritghs the Ministry of justice proposed to replace enprisonnement by other s maesures for petty crimes. Theses measure should emphazise rehabilitation.

In French cities, the buildings are a bit older than in other countries such as China or Duba. Firts, if you go to Paris, you will see a lot of old-fashion buildings downtown. They reflect the previous architectural French styles, such as Baroque or Renaissance. Buildings are not tall, and they are a lot of windows in each appartment. Then, if you go to the museums, you could have an idea about what France looks like in the past. The Louvre is a formal royal palace, and the Muse d'Orsay a former trainstation. Each of them have been refurbished to create a place where famous artists and exhibitions are host. I'd say that the most common line of the Parisians buildings are their windows, and their old-fashion look. Museums or houses are sometimes very similar, and it makes Paris as one of the most beautiful city in the world. A place where modern and old architecture can live together.

Dear Elizabeth, We have selected a ad campaign for Century watches. I'm sure you'll love our choice. Many people think that Century is a classic ans well-know brand, cause it has featured some of the most famous stars in advertising campaigns. While this might be true, it's also true that customers prefer Century because it's simply the best and most reliable watchmaker on the market. That's why we have managed to be one of the top worldwide. Considering Century's trend of working with famous stars, we contemplated the idea of bringing a celebrity in the campaign. However, we decided that the quality of the watch was more important than celebrity endorsement.  For this reason, we decided the ad would feature a man playing golf with a message not directed to stars ans athletes but directed to each and every man that will read and look at our ad. "Golf ans Century, The perfect team". Everybody should understand. I look forward to hearing from you. Best regards...
