text: Dear Mr. Smith, I wanted to inform you that we get the new client i talked to you last week. It tooks me a  lot of time to convince this french client that our services are exactly the ones he needs. After two meeting, we fixed it . His meaning of our quality is very good. As you know the french put a lot in havind good quality. The discussion of the prices was a bit critical, but afterall, he accepted them. A accorded them a 5% bonus. Before beginning our business with this company from Paris, we have to discuss several points. I planed to invit our new client to next lauch w'll have in November here in our headquarters. Let me know when it will arrange you so I can fix it with Mr. Dupont. I am waiting of your response. If you need more details please don't hesitate to contact me. Best regards, Julia Green

Green businesses do their part to conserve natural resources as much as possible. They may operate their machinery using renewable power  such as solar, wind or hydro  reducing the dependence on fossil fuels. Some environmentally conscious businesses install green roofs to positively affect energy consumption and provide a clean, vibrant break area for employees. Green businesses use organic, recycled, reusable or biodegradable materials as part of their business processes, such as furnishing the office or making copies; or to manufacture products, including food, paper products or packaging. People are increasingly attracted to the concept of sustainability. As a result, many go out of their way to support green business and buy green products. There are down sides to this trend, one of which is that some businesses claim to be green or sell green products even though their practices and materials have no environmentally friendly qualities. In doing so, they sacrifice ethics for profit. In fact, some people have become so disgusted by 'greenwashing' efforts that they are put off by the entire green industry.

When its about talking about renewable energy, my country seems always to be absent. Indeed our politicians think that we have bigger problems to deal with, like economic crisis. In my opinion all, if we make the good choice, renewable energy could be a trigger to our economy. I know that the first solution we have in mind for renewable energy is solar energy. Although it has a lot of advantages, he has one of the worse inconvenience: a high installation costs. So if we want to install solar energy, we shall invest a lot of money. For a country like ours it is a high brake. As far as Im concerned, I think we should choose a renewable energy like biomass. In fact biomass is relatively low cost and it just requires forest residue. Like you know we have a lot of forest. The government should just use his power to collect and reuse all forest residue in order to generate biomass.

I live near Paris, the nicest and the most beautiful town over the world. Beautiful because of the size of buildings and avenues. Tipically, buildings are six or seven-story high and were built in the mid 19th century. We have a curious tower designed by Mister Gustave Eiffel and built from steel in 1887.That became the most important symbol of Paris in the world. This old-fashioned Eiffel Tower is about 320 meters high with antennas. Nobody's living in. We just find a famous restaurant on the top and a lovely place to admire the beautiful townscape and to dream about a fabulous date with a really nice young woman. Moreover, we can see the Tour Montparnasse built from steel and glass. Its a dark glass building for business and housing situated near the main rail station inside of Paris. The tallest buildings are built in the area called La Defense outside of Paris. Most of them are business buildings for all kind of companies. Many modern towers were designed over there for a huge amount of business workers. This area makes you feel like youve been kidnapped by a strange community of people, over worked and over stressed, moving around a large esplanade like an antique meeting.%%

Buildings in France are an amazing blend of modern and traditional architecture. For example in Paris, you can admire the Louvre Palace which was built in the 12th century.The main building and its two wings are made of light yellow stones. They are surrounding a strange glass design reminding of a greenhouse without tomatoes : the Louvre Pyramid. Even if you are modern design friendly, you can't prevent from avoiding walking past it. In the heart of the city, when going for a walk, you can go past the big Hausmann buildings. Even if those rough apartment houses belongs to a luxurious era, they are lacking of refinement. In the same way, the skyscrapers in La Defense area are not very attractive. Considering the crowding of buildings,  the place I prefer in Paris is certainly the Trocadero Esplanade, a large area without any buildings, only some gardens and a few fountains. A place where you can stroll and rest from the crushing presence of the overwhelming buildings.

Hydroelectricity To answer the issue of nonsutainables energies ued in our city. I believe that hydroelectricity is the best way to participate in the world movement to save our planete against polution and to reduce the use of fossil fuel. Hydroelectricity is produced through use of the gravitational force of falling or flowing water. And with that, we can provide "green" electricity to all the houses in the city. To install that, we need a strong river with a dam built across It. I have imediatly think about the river which flow near the village. We have all what need hydroelectricity, why don't use it ? Hydroelectricity no required fuel costs and relatively low building and maintenance costs. With my research, i deduce that it will cost the city $75,000 ( one of the cheaper sustanable energy). To finnish, i believe that hydroelectricity can be a way to give a good image to the city ( an "eco-city"). Iam really concerned about saving the planete and you should be to!

I believe companies have a responsability to society, but like eveybody has. I mean, I cant stand the concept of irresponsibily, and Im deeply convinced that, whoever you are, whatever you do, unless you live alone in a remote island, you have a responsability to society. To my mind, the only difference lies in the spread of the aftermath. Therefore, the companies responsability is higher because they are more powerful, and all what they do impacts in a vaster medius. In one hand, companies cant dismiss their shareholders, to the extent that their money fuels their investments. Without them, they would belly up quickly and many employees would be laid off. Nevertheless, they must not consider only the shareholders interest. Too often, you hear about companies which shut down a factory in a country where workconditions are respectful of human dignity, in order to recreate it in another one, where children underfed will work at no cost, and in unsafe conditions. Thats outrageous. In the same way, it occurs that companies harm the environment deliberately to increase their profits. Thats outrageous as well. Therefore consumers must be vigilant and aware of their own responsability. Its up to everyone  not only to companies  that our world doesnt become a dog eat dog world.%%

topic: Writing about a disaster relief effort
text: Hello, As we spoke this morning, I made the few things you asked me for. It won't be long before I receive the first feedbacks from  our colleagues about what they would like to prepare for our boss's departure. God, I think you too can't even imagine how it will be withouth him in the company. He's here for years and he taught us everything. Anyway, I think it will be some kind of party at work, then we might invite Karl at the restaurant with us all and his wife. Carrie told me last thime I saw her he doesn't even imagine we could prepare such a thing for him. I can't wait,  We keep in touch,  Bye

topic: Attending a seminar on stress reduction
text: I choose again to discuss the similarities and differencies between the educational system in my homeland (Morocco) and Japan. If we just compare the infrastructures and the organization established in primary schools in Japan and those in Morocco, we will be shoked. Schoolchildren in Japan studies in electronic e-book and everyone of them have tablet so it can be easy for them to transport their school fournitures everyday. They also have electronic educative games that helps them to stimulate memory and curiosity to learn. In Morocco, each student have to transport on his back daily around 14 kg of books, and once he get to schools, he must search for a place to sit down among the large numbre of students (first challenge) and then listen to the teacher. Also, he has access to very few extracurricular activities which is not the case with his Japanese counterpart. This last, has also time to visit museum, do school trip to visit other countries and cultures. Comparing the incomparable make me sometimes sad, but this is the hard reality.

topic: Talking a friend out of a risky action
text: Hello everybody. As everybody knows, we are here today to celebrate the wedding of Guillaume and Julia. I would like to take a few minutes of your time to make a toast to this wonderful couple. Guillaume and I are long time friends and as some of you knows, we have always considered each other as a brother. I would especially thanks Guillaume and Julia's parents for this wonderful ceremony. I remember a time when we arrived at the airport at the end of our holidays, waiting for check-in when the hostess explained us that our number of flight was the good one but the plane took off the day before. Both of us were flat broke and we started a 2,000 Kms Hitch-hiking to go back to our home. Started a wonderful and surprising 2 days trip on the road. That's why I like him, a character balanced between spontaneity and fun. Always keep a good mood, whatever things are. Guillaume and Julia, I'm glad to be here, even proud, and I hope you the best for the future. To Guillaume and Julia !

topic: Hosting a group of foreign buyers
text: To start with this writing exercise , I would like to underline that I belong both to the French culture and to the Corsican one. Even though Corsica is a part of the French territory, it should be noted that the culture and the ways how people communicate between each other, are a little bit different from the French culture. In general people use both direct and indirect communication. It depends mainly on the context and the relationship between the persons. For instance, at work we use more the indirect speech to ensure friendly contact and polite relationships whereas at home we use more often the direct communication especially when brothers and sisters are speaking together. No need to soften the requests or to avoid conflict, we can be directly honest without hurting the listener. Concerning the body language and the gestures that are common in my culture, we can mentioned that the thumbs-up is really appreciated to encourage or congratulate someone. However the use or rising the third finger should not be used because it is seen as a really bad insult against someone both in French and in Corsica. The V-sign is also a positive sign but tapping your head is frowned up because it may have two contradictory meaning (positive and negative).%% Finally body language and gestures are more important in Corsica. You should keep a soft eye contact and a kind tone and everything would be perfect!

The picture of beautiful woman will be definitely our best argument. This kind of customers like the beauty of everything even if it is costly. Our market target will be associated with a soft song as jungle which will be disseminated in all radios. I propose to you to change the dress of that girl because it reminds us 'Marilyn Monroe' and we have to move with our time, maybe a brown girl will be more suitable. It could be great if we headlight the picture of the man who is totally attracted by this girl. The idea of Champagne is great to show how the party is lovely going and it demonstrates how people enjoy our products. I don't like the light in the left of the picture but maybe we could put a slogan with a picture of our product which will reveal what we sell.%% Keep it up!.

topic: Writing about a disaster relief effort
text: Dear Mr. X I have just received the complete file regarding the bid that you have done to the city of Bulka.I'm going to review it as quickly as possible. I'll give you by e-mail a complete legal advice next Wednesday, at the latest. I'll try to explain why they rejected your proposal from the perspective of the public procurement law. Meanwhile you must be aware that the legal framework has changed in France for the extra E-C companies since 2004. They have to justify that they comply with the social and environmental European regulation. Once, prices and quality of the tender were the main selection criterias. It's no longer the case. In order to be able to give you a pertinent advice, please send me before Friday your social report and any additional information which could bring to light that your company is involved in a socially responsible approach. Its critical that I receive all these pieces of information by the end of the week. If necessary, I can set up an appointment in our offices the second week of January to discuss the point. Could you let us know whether that's convenient for you?%% I truly appreciate your business and remain at your entire disposal for any question. Best regards

It is not a so old one, because it has been built on 1979 but on this place it occurs all the years in may the international festival of film. It is not nice because just built with concrete. If I speek about green business, for me it's the sale of products which are environment friendly. It also can be products which are produce in our area or country, not at the other end of the world. To be green I think the industrustry has to think about the packaging which often is too much for me. For example when I do my shopping , I always use a bag which I can be reutilised. I need my car to go to work every day but I try not to take it if not necessary. I also have a little garden where I plant  my own vegetables. I buy always tissurs which are recyclable. In my neighborhood many people have solar panel on their roofs; I taught to myself if I have to change my heating I'll also buy a green one which runs with air power and I'll put some solar panels on my roof too. I mean if a company built a new building, they also have to take care to utilise green material which are safer for our planet. My point is if everyone kate a little more care of what I buys and hopely what he puts in the rubish we can go ahead.%%

A lot of people would like to be successful but if so much people are writing about this topic, it is clearly because no one has found a recipe! It is true that one can observe some traits in a majority of successful people: be self-confident, motivated, creative and innovative... But it is not so simple. According to me, success doesn't only depends on you. The support of your family and your friends to believe in your capacities and to help you to realize your dreams is important. Then, if you are born in a wealthy or a successful family, it could be easier too. In this case, your family brings you the money you need to begin your project or can make you meet the people you need. Another point could be destiny and chance. Sometimes, you have the good idea at the good time or by chance you meet exactly the people you were needed to meet! This last point seems very important because in a lot of interviews of successful people they are talking about 'chance'... so, are you born on a lucky star?

It is difficult to pinpoint what are the typical traits leading to success. I could be more concrete by taking an example of a real success story. For instance, we could talk about my friend Nathalie, who recently opened a caf in our home city. That of course required courage to face the bankers and convince them to let her take a loan in their institution, but also passion to be able to work overtime every day of every week, in order to make it profitable. I think realizing this type of plan also require proactivity, because her project was very well planned. This goes ahead with the fact to be innovative because the initial idea, the concept, had to be brilliant. Moreover, creativity has to be highlighted, because she managed to set up a nice place with very little financial means and a lot of ressourcefulness. Let's hope it will be profitable !

What is a successful person? I can't make a complete and global definition. It depends of to many things.Some people believe that success and money are linked, while others consider that a successful person is a person who can help the others, everyone have his definition of success. But i am going to give you few aspects which are to me, the most important to be a successful person. In my mind, a successful person have inevitably to be motivated and passionated, because it is really hard to succeed in general and it necessite sacrifice. In the way of success you have to fail few times and learn obout your failure. A person who is not motivated, determinated or passionated will give up rapidly by discouragement. A successful person have to be competitive to because they are often other person who want the same thing than you and unfortunatly everybody can't succeed, it leads to an hard human competition. To me, a successful person is a person who follow and realize his dreams. And who is happy in his life.

When I left Bordeaux, I was twenty-one and very happy to say good-bye to this town that I hated.  My feelings were more addressed to my family than to the town itself. Now I feel rather like making a declaration of love to my beloved town! What happened? It is not only because it was listed on the UNESCO World Heritage a few years ago. There is a huge change in the way Bordeaux has taken advantage of its architecture. When I was young the town was not only dark but black; the river was brown and sad, you were prevented from seeing it because of the docks. Now, from the Garonne banks, recently pedestrian friendly, you can enjoy the wonderful sight of the Place de la Bourse as well as the long embankment faade a way to enter the 18th century, the glorious age of Bordeaux. The architecture of this century is sober: all monuments are on the same height (3 floors), same style, same soft stone. It is so beautiful to discover the Grand Theatre and the different avenues around that you can forgive, if possible, the origin of such a wealth: slavery and, later, wine.%%

For our city, we have to choose between five kinds of renewable energy: solar power, hydroelectricity, tidal energy, wind power and biomass. Our town is located in the countryside in the east of France and hasn't got any powerful river. That means that we can't use tidal energy because the seaside is far away. The same goes for hydroelectricity because of the lack of useful river, for solar power because of the insufficient sunshine. Wind power and biomass only left. Wind power could be an environmentally friendly and cost-effective solution due to the windy weather. But wind power farms require huge facilities which may injure the beauty of surrounding nature. So, as far as I'm concerned, I would recommend to develop biomass power. Our city is surrounded by large forests and crop farms. They could produce a lot of biomass with a good carbon footprint owing to the fact that it could be made on site. Required investment are relatively low. In addition to that, this way of using renewable energy would provide new openings to our farmer who are suffering from the reform of the common agricultural policy. In these circumstances, biomass would probably be the most relevant solution, both environmentally and economically.%%

Green energy is one of the major issues of this decade, or even of this century. In fact, with the world population growing at such a high pace, energy needs are increasing exponentially. We're now trying to replace nuclear and coil power by wind and solar power to get to a more sustainable world. That's what we call "green business". My opinion about green energy is that it is necessary to start using them massively, in order to preserve the Earth for the future generations of humans and animals that will receive the legacy we will give them. However, hypocritical measures are taken by governments and companies to give the impression that they are acting in that way, while they are doing only the very requiered things. I'm not really going out of my way to buy green products, because they're often too expensive, but I try to act every day in order to reduce waste. For instance I reuse plastic bottles, I buy products in biggest quantities to avoid getting many little packages, and I recycle paper and glass.

Paris is a town steeped in history.The city is well-known for its marvelously preserved historic architecture.Paris is a most tourist-visited city  in the world. In my opinion ,the cause of this success is maintain of balance between modern and historic arcitecture.For example ,the modern very busy district La Defense is relatively separate of historic center. At the same time there is a system of modern and effective public transport.The Parisian museums The Louvre and Orsay are well-known collections ,it houses as ancient as contemporary sculpture and painting.Paris is a very green city ,there are a lot of picturesque gardens and beautiful parcs here.The most famous is Jardin de Tuileries ,near the Louvre museum and Jardin de Luxembourg.I would like to recommend You to visit The Notre-Dame cathedral ,situated on Cit island.

topic: Attending a seminar on stress reduction
text: The differences between universities and "Grandes Ecoles" in France is difficult to understand for strangers. When I was post graduating law in Berlin, I spent much time trying to explain to my schoolmates what it meant. Having studied at the Universities and at a "Grande Ecole", I should have been able to tell clearly about it. But some didn't catch it because it's a little bit strange concept that doesn't exist abroad. Similarly, universities and "Grandes Ecoles" are teaching places which provide means for graduating. The main difference is that everyone is allowed to go to university, regardless of his school curriculum. Conversely to go to a Grande Ecole, you have to pass a very demanding exam which a lot fail. So the best students go to Grandes Ecoles" But unlike university, after having been selected, courses are very easy and there is time left to have fun without studying too hard. This different ways have both their advantages and drawback. The main problem is that it influences the whole professional life. The best jobs are, in fact, reserved for "Grandes Ecoles". It's always surprising to hear a CEO, 60 years old, who is still proud to be a past pupil of a famous "Grande Ecole".%% With the exception of Ivy league and Oxbridge, its a French uniqueness.

I want to tell you about the the famous monnument of Paris : the Eiffel Tower !. This masterful tour have 3 levels using for sightseeing. It's not an apartement building. Nobody,  lives on the Eillel Tower.. You can only go to the restaurant or for admire the view around. The last and highest level is situed at 279 metres and forbitten for public. It' s reserved for scientific and weather exprients. An TV and radio antennas top the spire. This breathtaking tower is place along the river Seine in the center of Paris in an attractive ancien area the Seven arrondissement. When it first constructed this was the most tallest of the world and it lasts over 41 years in the past.  Now Eiffel Tower is not in the running of the most tallest tower but may be the most famous over the world. This monumment is begaming the symbole of the capital of the France. and the most visited in France.

One the one hand, I think that it is a good thing to have superstores near your home, because it's useful and helpful, but in other hand  Internet and the superstores kill the small shops. Furthermore, in my area I can prepare my shopping by internet and I have just to pick it up in my car, what is definitely a time saving.  Nowadays, in the megastores they tend to phase out some jobs and replace it by machines what I am definitely against. A lot of people nowadays buy everything by internet because we can compare the price, they offer good services, good guarantee for the product  but after? What if you have a trouble with your purchase? How will you do to return it? The cost of the transportation still expensive, and it can be difficult to package it, if you had throw away the packaging. We have to make a balance between superstores and small shops because it is important to preserve the job  of people, and it is important to speak face to face, if you need some information and advices. Even if you have a huge description of the product by internet, you can't touch it and you can be duped by it.%%

The architecture of Paris has a really old story : it is a blend between old and modern architecture. Actually, the Paris urban area is the place where you can find the most important number of skyscrapers in Europe. They are located in three different areas : La Dfense, Italie circus and near from the Seine river. La Dfense is located in the western Paris area and gather the most important part of the French skyscrapers, it is so called the business district, the place where the main and most powerful companies have their offices. Italie circus is a residential district in the South of Paris, less than half a mile away from where I live. The third most important place is located along the Seine river, it is a blend between business and residential buildings. On top of that, there is of course the Eiffel tower, the symbol of France in general and Paris in particular.

topic: Writing about a disaster relief effort
text: Dear Santa Claus, I'm writing to update you on our progress regarding your different requests. First, from now on, as time is an important part of your business, we decided to forward children's letters to you every day, instead of every week. In addition of that, one person will help children in writting to you. Of course we will also send an answer from you to each of them. We really hope that will help you to be on time for your distribution of presents. Should you have any additional request, please contact me directly. Best Regards, Sylvie Virtu Mail Box Manager

Nowadays, crime in France becomes a tough problem, such as in many democratic countries in the world. Here, unless the government opens new prisons and recruits new staff, we can't deal with all the people incarcerated. Their solution actually is to let people with sentences lower than 5 years -even recidivist ones- in conditioned liberty. But the problem remains the same : we haven't got enough people to keep an eye on every criminal. To my mind, cutting down on the scurity budget is a really poor idea, even for political reasons. Maybe they should also think about forcing prisoners to build new prisons, with individual cells because... there is this second issue : I assume that most of the prisoners aren't hardened criminal and yet, they are often considered all the same. Then, those who made petty crimes are rubbing with dangerous people who must have bad influence on them. So, another objective should be to split up the upper form the latter.

Although green business is important to me, it would be a lie to say that it is a concept thats is dear to my heart. To be considered green, a business should be using organic and biodegradable materials and renewable energy such as solar, wind or water. These are the cornerstones of sustainability. However, to be truly green, a company should embrace social welfare, (cf. Corporate social responsibility) because humanity itself is a part of the world. It is not enough to treat the planet well, they need to treat people rightly as well. Unfortunately, I am all too aware of the increase of "greenwashing" in our society, which is one of the reason why I wouldn't go out of my way to buy organic products.  We can't take companies at their words anymore. Moreover, I also regret the fact that the products that come from well-know and respectable green businesses are so expensive. As a student, I, more often than not, can't afford them. Nevertheless, when I can, it doesn't bother me to pay a little bit more because there is always a good value for money.

The goal of green business is to encourage "green" practices among businesses. I mean, the first aim is to encourage people to be more environmentally friendly in order to recude and minimize impact of Human Being on nature. Green business is a concept developed to make people more responsible regarding to environment. These last years, within European union, a kind of ECO label has been developed. It aims is to avoid companies that pretend to sell ECO products where as they are not doing as much effort as necessary to consider themselves to be like that or, worst, by doing misleading publicity. To be honest, I don't go out of my way to buy green products but I try to respect as much as possible environment by doing simple tasks: take shower and avoid bath, turn off the tap when I brush my teeth, sort by kind of categories the garbages and so on...

One of the main reasons why Paris is considered one of the most beautiful cities in the world, is the continuity in its urban planning and architecture. I'm going to talk about two of its most strange and extravagant buildings. The Montparnasse Tower is a 210 meters office, one of the tallest skyscrapper in France. It is in the ideal location to discover all of Paris. It is the most central high point in the capital. Facing the Eiffel Tower, it is the best location for taking in the loveliest monuments of Paris. You can reach in it one of the fastest lifts in the world. You arrive at the 56th floor in less than 40 seconds. Situated at the heart of Paris, The Centre Pompidou reveals its unique, timeless and modern architecture. The outside of the building reflects the modernity that is held inside. The building's exterior is very bold: brightly painted pipes and ducts crisscross its transparence facade and an outdoor escalator flanks the building. To put it in a nutshell if you are into arts and architecture, you can't go wrong with Paris. It is one of a kind.

There are many persons I admire for their success in business: one of the best quality they have is genius: they started with a good idea or discover something and then succeed to deal with it and make the people believe in it, and buy it. By the way, genius or good idea is not always the key point: you can be good on that but then you have to be surrounded by good people, who will have to be well selected for their ability and skills. No one can succeed alone. It is true that people who look happy are generally well considered in the business world: you enjoy working with people who enjoy themselves, have positive energy and motivate people around them.

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: Incoterms are made to clarify international trade; that is, it defines clearly what the supplier's responsabilities are all along the transport of the goods and when the responsability is tranfered to the buyer, e.g. at departure (EXW), after being loaded on board of a boat (FOB), or at delivery, duty paid (DDP). It also clarify what the supplier is supporting in the name of the buyer, e.g. insurance and transport cost. Into EU, the incoterms are also used but generally simplified to only 2 over the 11 availables. Symply put, the sales are Ex Works (EXW) or Delivery At Place (DAP). Therefore, during the negociation of a sale with a customer, rather than spending time in the terms of the trade, i.e which responsablities are you offering to assume; you can focus on the additionnal services you wish to offer and indeed invoice. Look at it this way, EU offer a large market, with easy terms of trade for a more profitable activity. Mind you, EU is not the world and you should keep on trading out of.

I recently moved from up country to a city named Tours, in France. The city has history, every house, building even churches have history. Nowadays, almost every tower or house is located on a classified area. You can't change your windows without informing the city because it must not keep its look. Old, kind of like Asterix's vilage, there's a castle not so far from my home. The Basilic is being renoved, it's as high as any buisness tower in Paris, but it look more charming because every tower, house, even sidewalks, the go together. They fit like human body parts. It good to preserv a city's history, but when it doesn't allow the city to evolv, to develop itself because of certain manners the city tries to keep going with. It takes too much time to have a projet examined, and there's to many borders not to cross. You can't change your window like that, you have to make demands, you can't instal solar panols like that, etc... Too many rules kill the law. Even if the city looks great, what about the people living in it.

i'm currently working for a global company which employes ten thousands peaople in 30 countries. We have to prepare ech year a report on CSR. Only one persn is dedicated to compile actions done for the hole company. This dedicated person is coordinating the gathering of information from diffrent departments as Human resources, HSE, business and so on. In my opinion CSR is just a question of communication, this is not the business of a company to take care of the environnement or to save the planet. this kind of responsibility should stay in the hands of states or international organisation as ONU or dedicated ong. Company should demonstrate that they respect the rules implemented by states and if they want to make more they should have the possibility to donate money or support projects lead by governments.. To oblige companies to fill an annual report on CSR is counter productif, the result is a comunication exercice.

Uno is a card game. The rules are, every player starts with seven cards. The rest of the cards are placed in a pile face down. Next to the pile a space should be designated for a discard pile. The top card should be placed in the discard pile, the first player tries to match the card in the discard pile. You have to match either the number or color, for instance if the discard pile has a red card that is an 5 you have to place either a red card or a card with an 5 on it. If the player has no matches they must draw a card. You must lose all your cards, first player to do it is the winner.

We Mexican are famous for our bad etiquette. Few people say "please" and "thank you". We learn politeness and good etiquette in kinder garden, despite too many people do not practice them. People cut the line usually, and play loud music during parties. Macho attitudes are inculcated in Mexican males almost from birth, and machismo plays a pervasive role in shaping Mexican culture. Mexicans generally stand close together when conversing. Don't show signs of discomfort, which would be considered rude by your Mexican counterpart. Punctuality is expected of foreign businesspeople. Your Mexican counterpart may be late or keep you waiting. Thirty minutes past the scheduled meeting time is considered punctual by Mexicans.

Mexicans are extremely polite by Latin American standars. It is common to use the formal form of language (Usted) when you dealing with people you do not know or who are senior to you; for friends, close associates and informal situations, the informal (Tu) form of language is more appropriate. The use of formal language in informal situations in Mexico is often employed as a means to express irony. We ussualy say "thank you" when someone do something for us and "please" when we need help from others and they do that we want like a favor. We have special places to smoke, to play, for kids and for everything. Mexicans respect people of different countries we are very attentive.

When I was 23 years old, I was able to experience a big earthquake. It was a 7.2 earthquake in the Richter scale. I remember that I was eating at a fast food restaurant with my girlfriend, then all of the sudden we heard a big kind of cracking noise, and all the place started to move back and forth. All the people in the restaurant evacuated immediately, and we all stayed in the parking while the earthquake was still making everything shake. After the earthquake ended, we drove to my house, there was no electricity anywhere in the city, no cellphone service, there was no way to communicate. The electric company was able to reestablish the service by the next day, and we are lucky to say that no major damage was caused by the earthquake.

For a long time, books have been considered as the only "real" cultural artefacts, whereas media in general, and TV in particular, were regarded as popular means of leisure, without any real cultural value. But recently, audiovisual publications have been more and more recongnized as part of our cultural heritage. New patrimonial audiovisual libraries are being built, wich appear to be quite different from traditional broadcast archives. Users of such libraries are scholars, journalists, school teachers and pupils who perform what I call an "active reading" of audiovisual documents: they read and interpret these information in order to write and publish their own essays, assignments or articles.

And finally my recommendation when you are in other country try to read about its customs before arrive.

I live in Monterrey, Mexico. Travelers might be surprised at etiquette in our country, so I'd like to give some pointers. Here are things that you should and shouldn't do in Monterrey. Do: Men always greet each other shaking their hands. If you are a man, you should greet a woman with a kiss on her check. Bring a little gift when you're invited to a party or a dinner.  Leave the party early, but always after to help to the hosts. Don't: Talk with your mouth full. Spit on the street. Being disrespectful to women or elderly. Create problems in public places. I hope these pointers serve them in their future visits to Monterrey.

I was trying to buy a large refrigerator. My first step was to check models and prices of several brands in different departamental stores. Many of them had discount since 5 to 15% over the list price and also monthly payments from 6 to 18 months without extra charge. I decided to buy one of 19 cubic feet that way by 14000 mexican pesos, but they told me mi credit card was not included in that promotion. I get a little bit angry so I not buy anything.  After that, I commented that problem with my brother. He suggested buy it directly from the factory store . We went to that place, just outside the city and they offer 38% discount over the list price for a better refrigerator than those I had seen before. The payment was with credit card and delivered it at home in a week with an extra charge of 250 mexican pesos.  At the end, I buy a better refrigerator at prices 50% less than expected. I'm very glad with it.

Mexico is a beautiful country with different cultures in each region and has many touristic places which are visited by millions of people every year. If you are planning to visit Mexico is important you know the correct etiquette. When you are invited to a Mexicans house arrive 30 minutes later and bring a gift such as flowers, sweets or a bottle of wine. Arriving on time or early is considered inappropriate Take care with the kind of flowers you give because marigolds symbolize death and red flowers have a negative connotation, on the contrary white flowers are considered uplifting. If you receive a gift is good idea to open it immediately a react enthusiastically. Do not begin eating until the hostess starts.

This includes making or receiving calls, texting, playing games or web browsing. The speed limit is the maximum speed permitted on that road and you must not drive above the speed limit at any time. Some roads do not have visible speed limit signs, but speed limits still apply. You should be especially careful in residential areas and in school zones. Also, driving in rural and remote areas requires special driving skills and awareness of different road conditions.

Mexico is a great country. There are many places visited by millions of people of all world. The people are really friendly and warm. If you are invited to a person's home, these are some of the tips: Do If it is a convivial party, drinking alcohol in moderation. It will be very grateful for the host if you arrive with a small present. Hosts really appreciate the help you can provide in the event. Don't Initiate discussions about complicated topics like religion, politics or even some sports. It is very important ask your host if it is allowed to smoke in his home, there are some people who can not support the smell of snuff.

text: In my country there are strict regulations about driving and drinking alcohol. There's a program for all vehicules called "verificacion". It means that every six months, we need to go to an autorized center to check the car conditions. If all is okay, we obtain a decal to continue driving during the next period; if you don''t past that examination or you don't do the exam, you could get a expensive fine. Also when you are driving, you can not have drunk alcohol. There's a program called "Alcoholimetro"; it means that a police officer can stop you while driving and make a proof of alcohol. If you don''t pass it, you go to "El Torito" and stay there all day, before you leave it.

In my country there are many traditional shows and others coming from other countries. It is hard to say which is better because there are very good Shows these days. It is known worldwide as the dance of the deer, and this staging tells the story of a hunter goes into the forest looking for his next medal which is a great deer. Both the hunter and the deer are played by men wearing clothing according to their role. The story is wrapped in music and dances where second by second narrate how the story takes place. In Mexico manners are different according at geographic zone that are you living or visiting. In south region is a common practice offer a small gift on birthdays, gifts are related to gender. For example if you are visiting a woman, flowers are accepted, for a man wine will be ok,  in some places also small bucket of fruit. But in the north things are totally different, most common practice is a money on a birthday card is accepted. Visiting a friend house is formal to call  at least one day before, is not polite just arrive on the last minute. Other important rules are on a restaurant appointments. Is not polite to arrive late on any date, a lot of people think that arrive 15 or 20 minutes late looks good, but not. Other common practice on a restaurant is order a center dish for all the attendances, if you are thinking to do that just be sure that all people like the food that are you thinking, for example some people hate onions, others can't eat cheese.

text: REPORT: GENERAL EMPLOYEE SATISFACTION AT HANS AND SONS TERMS OF REFERENCE. PROCEDURE: The topics included satisfaction with: 1 Employee basic paclage: including pay scale, number of vacation days, sick days and overtime. 2 Employee advanced package: pension plan, health benefits and shares scheme. FINDINGS: 1 Employee basic package: -The employees were satisfied with pay scales, number of vacation days and sick days. 2 Employee advanced package: -Most employees were satisfied with the pension plan, health benefits and shares scheme. 3 Working environment: -Most employees were satisfied with office equipment. -Most complained that they didn't receive enough feedback. CONCLUSIONS: -Employees needs feeling more important at the company. -Need receive more appreciation of his achievements to feel comfortable at work. RECOMMENDATIONS:  -Remove some levels of management. -Introduce additional informal weekly feedback meetings. -Allow employees to become eligible for the advanced package after 18 months.

I remember a long time ago, I was living with my parents in Mexico, growing up with them. I was at second or third grade in school, I can not recall exactly. We, the students at the classroom were announced a performance was going to take place the next day. It was going to be a magician with a white talking rabbit. The time arrived and my mother had bought the ticket for me to enter and see the magic show. When I was in front of the show I could not believe I was looking at. I was like, Oh my god!. I really was so innocent, I was a young child. The magician was performing tricks of magic like, taking out doves from a hat, taking roses from I do not know where. I had never seen something like that in my life. I was really impressed and asking myself, Is it real? Is that possible?. Then, the magician also was a ventriloquy, you know, the man was speaking so that the voice seemed to come from a living rabbit. That was crazy and completely unbelievable for me at that age. How in the world a rabbit could listen to and answer questions?%% I thought. For me, the show was authentic, not only because the magician was an expert but costumes and choreography were fascinating, as well.

Many scientific reasearch have proved that smoking is very harmful to people. Because it is hard for people to get rid of this unhealthy habit, some countries have passed laws which ban people from smoking in public places and office buildings. In my opion, this policy is very necessary and worthy. It is the matter of our health. Some people say that they feel relived and relaxed when smoking in that it can help them forget their daily problems. Yes, this is one of smokings advantage. However, if one keeps smoking over a long time, he can grow lots of types of diseases. He might lose his strength gradually though hardly can he aware of this. He is easily affected by other severe diseases. And it costs him lots of money for treatment. Above all, he loses his health which goes and never comes back. Health is obviously the most valuable treasure in our lives.

In Peru, a South American country, there is still a gap of inequality in gender roles. Society still expects to shape the behavior of males and females differently. Women are supposed to be more patient, more faithful, more invested in family and relationships. In the other hand, men are expected to be the main financial supporters of their homes. This difference is even bigger in rural areas where is common to observe more traditional values. However, in the cities the generalization of education makes possible for men and women to obtain a degree and gain independence through a job. Many women in Peru raise their kids alone, so they are the main financial source of income for their families. There is also more representation of women running the government of the country, although until now there hasn't been any woman president. There is still work to do for gender equality.

One of the strictest laws in Mexico is the Anti-kidnapping law. Kidnapping is considered as a crime where a person is taken by force, intimidation or deception against their will to an undisclosed place. This law was updated recently in Mexico to prevent the great quantity of kidnappings that are occurring along the country. Due to kidnapping is considered as a serious crime, this law stipulates severe penalties for violation of its provisions and establishes a prison term of 20 to 40 years for anyone convicted, however if the victim is deprived of life, the prison term could be from 40 to 70 years. I think this law is very important because it has helped to decrease kidnapping victims due to criminals that know how severe will be the punishment if they are arrested, however the authorities have been overwhelmed by increases in violent crime related to narco-terrorist bands, while corruption and incompetence have originated a justice system weak which is extremely frustrating for all the people.

Nayarit, a small state, is located at the west coast of Mexico in front of the Pacific Ocean. The Occidental Sierra Madre cross the state through south-north, there on the mountains are living the Coras tribe. This people has ancient rituals to celebrates religious ceremonies, parties and all kind of festivities. The most important celebration take place on Holy Week, during the holy days: People has a religious ceremony afterward start to dance, drink alcohol, chew Peyote, since Thursday to Saturday. On Sunday they go to the Pacific Ocean to baptize children and to make offerings to the sea. The Chaman, a kind of priest and wish, is the ceremonies officer. Like many natives customes, there are a lot of mysticism and mysterious. The religion is a mixed of catolicism and their own believes. However, this people is peaceful and have many poverty. The women working making craftsmanship. The men go down to work in agriculture jobs. This story is not a performing art, but is a piece of culture whose characters are persons.

When you receive and invitation for a dinner party its proper to answer the invitation, no matter how time but it has to be before de date. If you accept to go, you don't have to bring a gift with you, tipically in Mexico all the celebrations o partys are paid by the host without any expectation of receive something. If you are going to a dinner party you have to arrive on time, if you are late, you must call or send a text message to apologize for the inconvenience. Once you are at the dinner party, you have to use silverware in the right way, avoid talking with mouth full and interrupt conversations. In Mexico we used to talk about general situations at the table, except politics and religion, this is seeking to prevent a discussion. Whe you think is time to leave, you must thank to the host for all the attentions.

I have to be thankful because my lifestyle is really quiet and with just a bit amount of stress. But some months ago, the things were not so. I had to work hardly long hours in a big, cold and stressful city and I felt burned out. However, an opportunity comes to move from that city to Cartagena, a smaller and warmful city. I changed my job to work as volunteer as part of a great educational campaign around the world. Actually, I don't get a lot of money but I have enough to get everything I need and even more. I realized that material things are not the most important in our lives and satisfying our spiritual needs is also important. As a result, I turned into a really happy person. My social life has increased because I have many good friends, so I don't feel alone. Also, I am living in the best neighborhood of Cartagena in the downtown, surrounded by beautiful historical places and houses full of flowers. I really enjoy my walking on its romantic streets. Now, I am sure what I want and I feel nothing is missing in my very meaningful life.%%

I am convinced I work for the best company all around the world. Why can I say it? Well, the office politics are based on deep roots of ethics principles. In fact, it has been our boss who has set the example and rules in behalf of the organization. One of the main statements in order to receive each other feedback is developing sincere love and empathy for the other employees. "Go and introduce yourself, remember when you came here" and "Share the best you have so that the other to get what he does not have " he says.  As a result, that has contributed to build up unbreakable, exceptional relationships among the coworkers. Nevertheless, sometimes difficulties and disagreements arise. Obviously, we cannot bury our head in the sand. Rather, we should apply these useful advices to overcome those issues: First, keep a regular communication with your boss; he has the power to be your biggest advocate. Second, seek the advice of an experienced colleague: There will always be someone who knows more than you. Third, be always well-informed: ignorance regarding some issues could make you a backstabber. Fourth, speak clearly, face-to-face, if you need to express your feelings, but do it with deep respect, don't humiliate or attack anybody.%% Last, love them: they are working with you shoulder to shoulder.

In the US, even in this day and age table manners are considered important. This applies even more when you're eating in a formal situation at someone's house. First of all, if you have any special diet requirements, your hosts will expect to be told about them in advance. Do not spring any last-minute surprises. Take a small gift with you. A bottle of wine, flowers or chocolates are all acceptable. Wait until your hosts being eating, or they say it's okay for you to do so; this shows that you are considerate. Don't make phone calls or send text messages while you are at the table. It's considered extremely bad manners. When it's comes to cutlery, the soup spoon is generally on the right, to the outside of the knife; the fork is on the left; and the dessert spoon is above the dinner plate. However, if you are not sure which of the silverware to use, you can employ a strategy I use myself when in similar situations: Watch what others do, and then do that, too. Good luck, and enjoy your meal.

In Mexico, even in this day and age, manners are considered important. Here are some important suggestions in case you will visit Mexico, some of this advice applies to another cities and remember, manners have to do with showing respect toward others. - If an elderly person gets on the bus, offer your seat, this advice applies to a pregnant women too. - If you travel in a car with a women always open her door and when she's completely inside close it. - If you visit a restaurant, always leave a tip. - Chew with your mouth closed. - If you visit a church, always take off your hat. - If you receive a visit, always offer food or drinks to your guests. - Always watch your language in front of children. - Wait to eat until everyone at the table is seated and ready to eat. - If you are visiting a friend and need to use his bathroom, remember to put the toilet seat down and flush the toilet. - Always make eye contact when you are talking with a person. I hope this manners helps you on your trip.

When I was young my family and I lived in Mxico City, but my mother used to take us to the Morelos State from time to time, in order to enjoy one of the most traditional dances for carnival named Chinelos. This dance was adopted in Mexico after the Spanish conquest as a native tradition into Christian festivals. I really used to enjoy very much the dance and the elaborated costumes. The Chinelos costume consists of a long adorned robe which usually has a rectangular shape, a mask made of mesh and a very expensive large colorful plumed hat. The dance is a set of repetitive steps and the choreography is very simple where the dancers perform two shuffling steps and then with one shoulder or the other, take a small jump to the right or left; but the best is that the dancers are always accompanied by a wonderful brass band. Most Chinelos dancers are young man and their role is passed from parents to children during many generations. Nowadays I continue enjoying the Chinelos dance when the celebration of my neighborhood takes place every year.

Five years ago, my wife and me took a vacation on a cruise and went to the Greek Islands. These trips last about 7 to 15 days according to the Islands you want to know.The ships are enormous with 2000 passengers capacity or even more. The passengers are men and women and childrens of all kind of nationalities. There are many guides of tourists who'll take the tourists to the land tours aboard buses to know the archeological sites and important places as well as the usual places of the island like the stores, the market, the church and the beautiful landscapes. At the end of the day, people comback to the ship at a certain hour to have a supper and enjoy the musical shows with singers and dancers. Then people going to sleep because the ship going on during night to another island, and at the next morning the trip goes on. But the awkward situation ocurred at the fourth day when we woke up at 6 a.m. and after the shower, dressing and the breakfast, get down the ship and aboarding our bus, my wife suddenly told me ' I forget the purse with the money and the credit cards on the cabin under the pillow !%% 'My Godness! 'What a problem' At that moment we felt so embarrassed and made a decision to get down the bus and comeback to the ship to look for the purse. Fortunately, we found it. We lost the tour on that day but the awkward situation had a happy end.

It was an awkward situation that happened yesterday. All started the day before yesterday when a colleague realizes that there was a problem with a production line. This problem was serious and one of the options was to stop the production line to avoid wasting millions in raw materials but in the other hand it also implies to lose millions in sales. When my colleague was telling me the information he was as worried as Id never seen him before. When I receive the information, my first thought was stop the production line but then I realized it wasnt my right to take this decision alone. By the end of the day the note had spread out through the whole company but none wanted neither to make the information official nor to take a decision. Finally somebody give the order to stop the production and it was like lighting a match in a gas station. Today all the options have been analyzed and we are just about to take a decision but the situation wasn''t as awkward as the first person who gives the order had thought it was going to be.

Im 26 years old, and that is not enough to see as many changes as someone older than me would see. For example, in the 90s, no one talked about e-books and most of the people kept on the top of the news watching TV or reading newspapers. Nowadays, a new generation uses more often electronic devices: A high average of the population in some countries carries lots of books in their tablet, others read in internet on their smartphone instead to buy books of paper. Doubtless, the technological advances has changed the habits and the points of view of many people today; many consider that books of paper are old-fashioned and don not take care of our environment, so they prefer to read on their laptops, smartphones and tablets. However, something is surprising and worrying me: Lately, I have left my books of paper because I am reading more often on my laptop! I want to take advantage of the technology, but the most I spent time trying to read on my laptop, the less I read my loved literature and poetry. Some years ago, the government of my country established a law that doesn't allow smoking in any public place. Neither bars, restaurants nor closed places as offices and stores. In my view, it was a great idea. Since the law took effect on 2008, the consumption of cigarettes has gone down dramatically. Many of my friends and colleagues quit smoking. In their opinion, outdoors smoking is not comfortable and enjoyable because is a social habit. Other people used to smoke inside buildings for studying or working in order to get concentration. As every Mexican, I love the Mariachi, it has a long been considered a uniquely Mexican sound, representing a homegrown tradition that embraces both indigenous and foreign elements. They originally played only with strings instruments dressed in the white pants and shirts of peasant farmers; now they performers playing a variety of stringed and brass instruments and are dressed in expensive Charro outfits. Modifications to the original music include influences from other music such a polkas and waltzes, in fact they included the rock and roll, salsa, etc. The mariachis singer is very important, because he transmits all the feelings of the orchestra and of the song. The most famous singer of this kind of music is Vicente Fernandez. You can enjoy this kind of performance in every city of the country; some places offers a free show. If you are listening the Mariachi, you should take a less a tequilas drink.

I was born northern Mexico, but since almost five years ago I live in Mexico City. Mexico City is awesome, but it has to deal so many problems too. In this case, we need tough laws. One of the biggest problems we have right now is pollution. Vehicles moving from side to side of the city, generates high pollution levels. Government do its part fighting against pollution. For example, we have a government program called "Eco-bici", in wich you will take any bicycle from designed 'stations' to move to a new place in the city and left this bicycle in another station. Also, we have a law that prohibits to take (using your car) your children to school. They have to use the bus-school-service. Private schools are required to offer this service, and its paid by students. Public schools are not required to do so, because neither the parents nor the government have the enough money to cost the service. Despite the downsides, from a political point of view, this law helps getting lower pollution levels, even who spend the money for paying for the service.

In Mexico manners are considered important, in meetings and social events you have to know how to behave. If you are visiting Mexico for work these simple pieces of advice could help you when you are invited to eat. When you are invited to a dinner or have lunch, do not talk about business because the meeting is a mere social gathering to get to know but not to talk business. At least you should not do it until they do so first . If the invitation is for breakfast, probably it is a working meeting. Dinner is more social and less likely to talk about business in it; when the dinner is in the hostes's house is a good manner to bring a small gift, typically something to eat or drink during the same dinner. If the break fast, lunch or dinner is outdoors it have to be paid by the person who invited, and you should not try to pay the bill or offer to pay a part of it. You may correspond to this gesture with another similar invitation.

The most effective or important management strategies for a business are: 1. Everything is constantly changing market conditions, the business environment, consumer spending habits, advances in technology, and more, so "embrace change". 2. Lead a company, don't over-manage it, some managers try to do everything and force its employees to work regardless of their opinion, this make employees without initiative and dissatisfied with their work. 3. Provide immediate feedback based on decisions made, so the employees feel that they are taken into consideration. 4. Analyze where your business stands in relation to your key competitors and what the future will look like. 5. Establish objectives to answer unmet needs in the marketplace, taking both a long- and short-term view of what your company can offer. 6. Give Motivational leadership by concentrating in achieving sustained performance through personal growth, values-based leadership and planning that recognises human dynamics. 7. Detail the goals the company has for itself, both in terms of financial milestones and strategic objectives.

Colombia is a beautiful country. It has a endless variety of animals, plants and places to visit. But it is not the exception when we talk about social problems. Hence, we are aware of the importance of laws so that everyone of the citizens and their environment be protected. Because of that, recently a law about smoking in public places has been created. The law stipulates that nobody is allowed to smoke in bars, restaurants and in the parks in some cities. It was expected that people who smoke would speak against the law and they consider it very strict. In fact, some of my friend would wish it could be more leniant. Obviously, anybody wants his freedom be shortened. But, sometimes someone's freedom means other's slavery. Many of us do not want to be slaves by nicotine and dangerous substances for the health. As we know, scientific researches have proved a numerous diseases produced by the cigarrettes. Doubtless, this law is quite beneficial, specially for the children who hang out with their friends in the parks. Personally I totally agree with the law. Also I agree that careless and disobedient people be charged because smoking in public places is an infrigement on our main right: the life.%%

text: Developing leadership qualities There is a lot of controversy about leadership: while some people think that leaders are born, not made, others think the other way around; myself, I think that nothing is permanent, and that leadership skills can be taught or developed. Who I admire the most because of his or her leadership characteristics? Well, there are some very different people, but they all have some things in common: in the first place, they really care about others -not use them, and, in second place, they all have a vision and work hard to reach their goals. Regarding leadership, there are some misunderstandings, because some people think that leaders are those that speak aloud, scream or manipulate others, but real leaders dont have to be very visible or very obvious, they can guide others quietly and leading by example, not just with empty words. True leaders give credit to those who work with them and help their collaborators to put their strengths to the service of the team in order to achieve a common goal.

Judith Wight, 'How to Make a Career Out of Courage'. Some people are discontent with their job. text: There are many kinds of extreme sports. However, which I prefer is downhill. Why do I prefer thhis sport? There are three main reasons that answer the question. First, the equipment needed to practice downhill is incredible. The bikes are big but light at the same time. Furthermore, it is necessary to wear an special outfit. For example, it is compulsory to waer a helmet and a set of knee and elbow pads. In addition, this sport requires an excellent physical condition. You have to have an extraordinary muscle strength, therefore it is mandatory to practice a wide range of exercises at gym. Finally, to practice downhill you must have nerves of steel. In other words, as you have to ride your bike downward from the peak of a mountain, as fast as you can, you should be extremely focused in order to not make any mistakes that could make you fall from your bike, thus, probably leading to severe injuries. In conclusion, downhill is may favorite extreme sport because it demands an specific equipment, hard training and nerves of steel.

When you invite a girl for a date it's polite to hold the door open for the lady. In the restaurant she shold sit first and the man should put the chair of the woman if the correct place to sit her. If you have a bottle of wine usually the man taste the wine first, althoug if the waman ask for she tates firstly that it is ok. After that the woman must be served her glass first. Before starting eating someone wold say good appetit', then the others repeat the same. You shouldn't left the table before all the others have finished (almost you have an emergency -- for example to go to the restroom ---). You shouln't have to put your elbows on the table. Traditionally the man pay the check. Usually you lend a charity (tip) for the person who serve the meals if you assist to a restorant, that's ok if you give 10 to 20 percent of the bill.

As many countries in the world during 1990''s, the roles of women and men have changed a lot in Mexico. At the Mexican Revolution, women demonstrated that they could perform activities that to those days were considered exclusive to men, including go to the war. At that time woman was seen only as a man belonging, which only served as a company and to procreate. There was no gentlemens. The acknowledgement of the right of women to vote was the most important event that marked the change and led to more social struggles in pro to equal gender. Improved access to information and feminist movements eventually ended up closing the gap that existed between genders. Currently in Mexico gender discrimination still exist, especially in the more remote areas. But most of men treat a women as ladies with equal rights.

In Mexico we have lot of different dances from every region of the country, in particular I really like a typical dance of the state of Veracruz, called "El baile de los viejitos" in which a group of people dress up with zarapes above white suits and red ties , putting on an old facing mask and a hat and using a walking stick to represent old men that hit their walking sticks to the floor and dance in circles. I also really like the plays that Mexico City has, especially Mexican style pastorelas that contains playfulness, black humor and lots of comedy, this performance normally carried out considering the current situation of Mexico, making a constructive criticism sometimes of the current government and sometimes of gossip about artists. I also used to be a rude person when my colleagues asked me about a few things like: do you have a girlfriend? or are you married?. Or for the worst: how come you dont have a girlfriend? Im gonna look for someone for you. At the end, I ended up yelling at him and leaving immediately. Now, I understand the importance of getting along with your friends. After a few advises from my boss, I started working on myself. It is hard for me to change, because Im also a resentful person. But, thanks to my boss, things are getting better over the time. Another thing she suggested me, is that I have the skills to run my own business. Now Im working on my business plan, and soon I will open my animation company.%%

In my city we have a dance school that is one of a kind because dance is an art form characterized by the human body as a vehicle of expression. There are soccer and tae kwon do schools, but what makes dance unique is that provides opportunities for the development of critical thinking and analytical skills, improves the development of self-expression and it helps to communicate emotions through movement and music. Dance has been described as an exciting and vibrant art which can be used in the educational setting to assist the growth of the student and to unify the physical, mental and emotional aspects of the human being. When I was a little girl I studied at that school for three years, and for me, there''s nothing like dance, and the choreographies can be unrivaled. Even more, in my opinion, dance is accesible for most people; no special equipment is needed, just the ability to move...

topic: Buying a painting for a friend
text: Certainly, all people do not have leadership skills. Even I was not born with these. But it can be developed if we do all we can to become confident leaders and set a precedent: whoever wants will be successful despite the difficulties. Regarding hardships, one of the things we should learn is overcome our own weaknesses. How could we do it? First at all, it is vital to be honest with ourselves and recognize what areas in our lives we have to change or improve. Afterward, looking for useful books related with the topic or asking for advices from people with experience in overcoming challenges can provide practical tips. Of course, changes are hard in the beginning, it makes us stronger and shows how we are able to be adaptable. Also, other critical thing is having meaningful relationships with others. Remember: nobody can do all things by himself. We need us one another. Receive, then, the feedback from your coworkers: instead of set your sight only in their weaknesses and faults, try to put yourselves into de other's shoes, it means, be empathetic. Although this is demanding in the way, we will be exemplar leaders for sure.

Usually, we don''t realize how lucky we are of being able to laugh, to walk, to move; we don''t realize how precious is the health we have until we see close to us someone suffering from any kind of sickness. Dear Mr McDonald, I'm writing regarding an issue of breach of contract. As far as my claim for overtime payment is concerned, my contract clearly states that I should receive compensation for weekend work. Furthermore, nothing in the contract allows the company to refuse to pay me for something that isn't your fault. In this point, you are taking much in the moral high ground. Consequently, TransWorld Enterprises must pay me for working the two weekends and I believe I am within my rights to object to being refused overtime pay. If I don't receive overtime pay, I intend to sue TransWorld Enterprises for damages. Yours sincerely, Gabriela Garza.

In the Mexico, even in this day and age, table manners are considered important. This applies even more when you're eating in a formal situation at someone's house. First of all, if you have any special diet requirements, your hosts will expect to be told about them in advance. Do not spring any last-minute surprises. Take a small gift with you: A bottle of wine, flowers or chocolates are all acceptable. Wait until your hosts begin eating, or they say it's okay for you to do so; this shows that you are considerate. Don't make phone calls or send text messages while you are at the table. It's considered extremely bad manners. When it comes to cutlery, the soup spoon is generally on the right, to the outside of the knife; the fork is on the left; and the dessert spoon is above the dinner plate. However, if you are not sure which of the silverware to use, you can employ a strategy I use myself when in similar situations: Watch what others do, and then do that, too. Good luck, and enjoy your meal!

Growing in the Mayan culture of Guatemala, I remember see my first Mayan performing art when I was 9 years old. By that time I had no idea what 'The Dance of the Leopard' was, and it was difficult for me to understand the deep meaning of this traditional play, let me tell you that this play has been inherited from hundreds of previous generations, and how enjoyable is to watch such a dramatic, energetic performance. The performance begins in a wood setting with shadows casting everywhere on set. The magnificent, and living music transports audiences to the wonderful 50 B.C in the north of Guatemala. Suddenly, people wearing colorful costumes begin to dance, while leopards, that are people wearing costumes, come on stage, and join the amazing choreography. There is nothing like 'The Dance of the Leopard' in any part of the world. The story is the fascinating life of the interaction of the Mayan civilization, and the god of nature that gave birth to the leopards which they considered sacred.

There are many rules of etiquette and good manners that we should demonstrate in a meeting and it would be very extensive talk about them, because they go according to time and opportunity , but the most common and important to show a good education in Mxico are the following . Getting to the meeting five minutes before the appointed time , if the meeting is in the day and avoid wearing a suit if the meeting is outdoor , dress informally, but if it is a dinner invitation,  you must dress very formal. When you arrive you should greet with a handshake to the host. In Mexico the greeting kiss on the cheek is not common ( because of the influenza epidemic that we suffered some years ago). When the time comes to go to the table, you must wait for the host will point to your place. You should also avoid smoking during dinner, neither talk with your mouth full of food and do''nt talk about topics that may be uncomfortable for some people such as , religion, money and politics . Never get into discussions of opinions even if you do not agree with what is said at the time. Always you must try to be tolerant.%% When the meeting ends . To thank their hospitality and comment by the pleasant moments during your stay, as well as delicious was the dinner.

Manners and etiquette for dinner party are a little bit complicated, but theme are necessary to show respect toward others. In Colombia table manners are considered important, when people attend formal invitations, the idea is show a good behavior, arrive on time and always take a small gift that represent your gratitude, for example: A cake, a bottle of wine, or chocolates. While people are taking dinner the host expect that the guest eat all and avoid bad manners, for example: don''t put your elbows on the table, don''t talk with your mouth full, don''t use your phone during the dinner and don''t do uncomfortable sounds while chewing. In our culture we don''t expect receive a thank-you note. Before finishing dinner the guest invite the host to have other special occasion, but in his or her house. If you have troubles with how use the right cutlery, I advice to start with cutlery that is outside the dish and continue until arrive to the cutlery that is close the dish, or just be relax and imitate other person.

The dance of the flyers or pole flying (voladores de Papantla in Spanish) is a unique and risky performing art to Mexican culture. Nowadays is considered as an Intangible Cultural Heritage by UNESCO. The performance consists of 5,4 or 3 men that fly around a 30 meters pole while one is standing in the top of the pole playing the flute. These men wear colorful costumes that are made by artisans women with the traditional colors and figures from Aztec, Otomi and Huastec roots. Like I said before this performance is quite risky, because the flyers do not wear any kind of protection while they are revolve around the pole by a rope fastened. It is a beautiful and engaging performance that many people around the world come to witness and love to be part of the audience with the beautiful music, vivid colors and warmth of the inhabitants.

In Mexico, etiquette and manners aren't as important as in other countries,  such as the UK or China, for example.  If any, it deppends on the situation. For instance, if you are invited to a reunion with friends or family, you are expected to feel as if you were at your own home. If its a business meeting,  its impolite to be too stiff or not friendly with the person you are going to do business with. Mexicans are very friendly people, so manners are all about expressing that to others. One thing that you have to be very careful about is when it comes to giving thanks to your host. People in Mexico consider impolite not to say Thank You when you are a guest or have been presented with a gift or something. So don't forget to give thanks!
