At my country there is a law relatively new, against drinking alcohol and driving automotor vehicles. The Mexican authorities have implemented aleatory examinations to drivers by Alcohol Measurers authorized by government (Intoxylizer 400AP) which measuring the breath of driver and determine the alcohol concentration in blood. The maximum level permitted is 0.4 mg of alcohol in blood. The examination places are outside bars and restaurants and other places in aleatory way. You can drink approximately five standard drinks (15 c.c. of ethanol) at five hours, about one standard drink  per hour. If you have a higher level during the examination you can't drive and go to jail for 12 to 36 hours without appeal right. The Mexican Society has accepted this law because has diminished considerably the amount of car crashes and the mortality rate owing fatal accidents and the number of serious injuries at young people.

In the U.S. there are thousand of people who expresses their different opinions through different means. This is a right that anybody can enjoy of called freedom of speech. However, this right is regulated by laws that protect people from defamation. What these laws contemplate is that any people in the U.S., and many countries share these laws, have the right to discuss, speak, talk, and so forth about any topic; however, defamation is a crime. In my view, the laws about defamations not only creates a safe environment to self-expression, but also protects people's image. All of this is clear when we observe countries like China, and Venezuela where people lack of freedom of speech, and therefore, the country becomes an environment of defamation in which truth is no more than a ghost.

The Scene: the Centro Nacional de las Artes (CENART), Eurojazz has become one of the citys best musical offerings. The month-long festival highlights a dozen or so different new jazz talents from as many countries throughout the continent. Shows take place at CENARTs open-air Plaza de las Artes at 5pm on Saturdays and 4pm on Sundays (and some Fridays) throughout the month of March. Recent performers include Belgiums Jef Neve Trio; Polands Pink Freud, which fuses jazz, contemporary rock, and electronica; as well as the Netherlands funk-driven Rare Groove Orchestra. Now going into its 12th year, Eurojazzs attendance has grown exponentially. Last year, more than 9,000 people were grooving to the Czech Republics James Band. All shows are free. Hot Tip: Even though festival promoters have moved show times to later in the afternoon (they used to be at 1:30pm), the Plaza de las Artes still gets tons of sun. Bring a comfy cushion, hat, sunscreen, water, and a snackfor some odd reason, CENARTs restaurants are closed on weekends.

in my country of origin (Libya), even in this day and age, etiquette and good manners are still considered important by many people. For example, it is supposed for a man to hold the door open for a woman. When two couples go out for the dinner, it is expected a man who pays a bill. When you invited to someone's house, it is supposed to bring a small gift like a box of chocolate. Table manner is important; you cannot talk while you are eating or your mouth full, you cannot eat before the host or the hostess sit and start eating and you have to place the napkin on your lap once you sit on the table. Furthermore, you should to thanks the host for all her or his efforts for preparation such a dinner. It would be much better to return this invitation for the host in the future at your convenience.

Dear Valeria: Im glad to know you received the invitation card. As I told you, you know they are about to get married and even though the party is going to be quite small and familiar, there are some rules of etiquette that you can bear in mind. Also, in regard with the customs of our culture, it is very important to know what kind of manners youre expected to show. First at all, the hosts want us to be on time to enjoy all what they have prepared. When you arrive, greet first to the elderly ones and ask them how they are. That has to do with showing respect toward the experienced ones; we highly estimate such kind of behavior. Moving forward, typically, were expected to wait the hosts to be seated before starting to eat. Do not become stressful about using the cutlery or putting the napkin over your lap or that kind of staff because it is not required in this party. On the other side, you know a gift is not required but it would be thoughtful and lovely from your part to bring something typical from your country to show your best wishes to them, specially we enjoy gifts from other places.%% Finally, please confirm as soon as possible if you are going to attend. I am looking forward for your reply.

Almost everywhere, solid majorities express support for gender equality and agree that women should be able to work outside the home. Most also find a marriage in which both spouses share financial and household responsibilities to be more satisfying than one in which the husband provides for the family and the wife takes care of the house and children. In addition, majorities in most countries reject the notion that higher education is more important for a boy than for a girl. Yet, despite a general consensus that women should have the same rights as men, people in many countries around the world say gender inequalities persist in their countries. Many say that men get more opportunities than equally qualified women for jobs that pay well and that life is generally better for men than it is for women in their countries. This is especially so in some of the wealthier nations surveyed. And while majorities in nearly every country surveyed express support for gender equality, equal rights supporters in most countries say that more changes are needed to ensure that women have the same rights as men.

Im writing regarding your request for advice on a breach of contract by your employer, TransWorld Enterprises. Furthermore, nothing in the contract allows the company to refuse to pay you for something that is not their fault  talk about taking the moral high ground! Office Politics Some years ago, I used to work in a business advisory firm. Most of the people were hardworking and very focused on satisfying the needs of their clients, but, of course, there were what is called office politics, a game I didnt know how to play very well at that time, but, nevertheless, I survived the modern jungle thanks to Richard, a former boss who taught me how to behave wisely in order to accomplish my goals. Since it is difficult to understand how rude the play could be, let me give you some examples of what Victoria, a coworker used to do: go to visit the clients of the other consultants and ask them about every possible problem they could have been experiencing, then writing a partial report and informing the director about it, mentioning that the clients were really upset with the service provided by the consultants. She also used to schedule meetings at last hour in the afternoon when other people were about to leave and if they refused to attend, Victoria was gossiping about consultants lacking motivation and leaving early every day. Obviously, she didnt forget to mention she were all the opposite.

In my country getting a job isn't easy, there are not enought jobs, besides, taxes have gone up significantly the last years. Luckly for me that's not a problem. I am married and I have a baby. My husband is employed in a recognized company here in Mexico called Pemex, wich provides him and his family ( in this case me and the baby ) with extra benefits like medical insurance, a retirement plan and more, so I could say that we're in good shape financially. Our money management is good, our income is about 1500 dollars per month, wich would be 20 000 pesos here in Mexico. The biggest part of our monthly expenses is the food and transportation, because we don't pay a rent or housing, we're currently living with my dad, so he takes care of that until we move into our own house next month. It won't be that easy once we begin living on our own, but we'll get through it because my husband is really thrifty, that's the most important thing if you want to get by.

I always thought the politics in the office was unnecessary, because the main goal is how to construct the process to make more profits and to serve to our community. I saw some of my colleagues doing dirty things, stabbing in the back, just to make them feel their power and forgetting the projects and the job what they have to do. But I was so wrong, the office politics is really important. Every relationship between people has to have politic behavior. The human being is by nature, sociable, religious and politic. Indeed we have to consider the office politics. You can achieve the knowledge from your partners without being deceptive or offensive. Instead, show them respect and they will help you to solve your problems. Understand what your bosses want and he or she can be your advocate. However, it doesn''t mean that you should compromise your ideals, is just to figure out how to make them do what you want or what has to be done.

I'm happy with my life, however, there are few things that I would like to change. I'm a veterinarian, and despite what other people think, it is a very stressful job, you have to take care of animals, and I mean human animals and no-human animals, I have to treat with crazy people and aggressive dogs and cats and that is exhausting, in a physical and mental way. But there are very good things too, like I'm my own boss, I can spent time with my family, I can go to the movie theater and I can do the things that I love most. Although is the job that I always wanted, I'm looking forward to do something else, I still don't know what I want to do in a near future but I have a few things in mind that I always wanted to do. For the moment I just want to maintain the calm and do my best for my family and my work.

Gender differences in my country have improved (Mexico), but there still too much work to be done. I see everyday more companies who try to provide the same opportunities for both genders and I see women taking bigger roles and positions in the companies. I think the day when Mexico will have its first woman as President is not too far.

In Mexico people are very sociable. Usually, colleagues or friends invite you to a dinner party or casual meeting. When you go to someone's house for dinner, it is expected that you take a small gift like a bottle of wine, small dessert or an appetizer. Also, people are expected that you eat all food that your host serves to you in order to recognize that is delicious. It is a good manner to offer help to your host (for example: put bread on the table). On the other hand, even when Mexican know that are bad manners, it is common to arrive late for a meeting, to invite someone else (take with you a friend or friends) and use cellphones on the table. Likewise, it is considered bad manners chewing with you mouth open, putting your elbows on the table and intentionally burping or passing gas. Unless the host asks you, people are never expected that you take off your shoes before enter a house.

In my country as many countries in the world you can't smoke in the bars or in the restaurants. This is a new law, I mean, we have only five years sense it was published. How the people reaction to this??? Well, we have a lot of opinions. Most of the people are happy with the desition. Now, they can go to a restaurant or even to a bar and breath cleaner air. When they get to home, their cloths are not stinking, But in other hand, there are people who are habituated to smoke. For them, has being very difficult and very noying have to move (going out side of the place) to smoke, a cigarette, a pipe or a cigar. In my opinion I am agree with the new law. The Ibero-American theater festival, is unique because is the biggest performing art in Latin America. Tourists and Colombian people enjoy it once every two years. It takes place in Bogot, that is the capital of Colombia and these performances are performed on the street, where pedestrians can feel the magic and see others cultures, because the festival invites the most important theater companies from five continents; they bring many shows, which you can see inside on a theater that is a bit expensive, but well worth the money or outside, that is free. For a week the capital lives in a superb party. The festival offer the most complete panorama of performing arts. Additionally, there are specialized workshops and lectures by maestros of the scenic arts from five continents. Every two years I hope anxious  this event, besides  to be marvelous, it is in the Easter week and in this period  frequently I don''t have anything useful to do.

Small cities have a lot of traditions, some of them include to give to the groom, the bride, by a lot of money. In other cases, it''s a custom to realize big parties for weddings. In big cities, many women doesn''t like the idea of a wedding, they prefer to live together, not just married.

Do we still think about gender differences? There are not much gender differences here in Mexico. In my country it's typically proper to bring a gift when you attend to an invitation, but shouldn't be an expensive gift because can make the host feel uncomfortable. It's consider good manners when you arrive to the hostess's house to wear formal clothes instead of wearing shorts, miniskirts or wearing caps. You should wait for the host, before starting the dinner and while you're eating, you never have to attend phone calls, text messages or talk with your mouth full because can be consider disrespectful and it can be consider rude for all the guests. Don't talk very loud while you talk to the other people, don't drink too much alcohol, never leave the host's house late, when you see that everybody starts to leave the house then leave, you have to do it too. Don't make bad commentaries from other people whos not invited that could make feel uncomfortable to the others and some of them may know that person. If you follow this directions correctly you may be consider a person who keeps good manners and people may show respect to you.

Since I was a young my parents taught me how to save money, they always gave me a good example to save and invert my money in useful things; for instance buy clothes, pay utilities, go to a good budget supermarket and enjoy my time. Obviously when I was young I did not have many debts. I always gave some gifts for birthdays and Christmas, for this reason I saved money. Nowadays I am employed, but i still need to be thrifty, I get a paycheck twice a month, but the company pay me 40 percent per and 70 percent. This is a big problem because I have to pay my bills in the first fifteen days a month; so I need to get by with this salary and method. Other problem is that my company not provide me benefits, so I am thinking to resign,  I will be in the unpleasant circumstance to be unemployed but I need that things turn around. I wish pick up some work!

A performing art that is unique in Mexico is called "Charreria", that it is considered as the national sport. This show is based on the traditional activities of the animal husbandry, and it shows some skills with the rope, charros riding horses and charros roping cows. It is a really exciting performance to watch! And the costumes that charros have to wear are just exquisite. This show requieres an open mind, because it include animals. But is different than bullfighting, because in charreria animals are not killed. There are some really dangerous tricks, but the Charros make them seem really easy to do. Woman are also part of these events, they are called "Escaramusas". Escaramusas do a lot of different acts over the horses, riding them sidesaddle, wich comes from an European origin. It is a great show, there are really brave people in it, and is really unique. You can find these acts in Guadalajara or Mexico City.

At my country,manners and etiquette are important today. There are different social events; dinner party, wedding dinner, school graduation and others. Let's see a wedding dinner. It supposed previously,you have received an invitation to you and your family.  It.s polite you reply quickly to an invitation. When you arrived to the reception room, you should wait to he hostess to come in and sit on the place assigned to you. After greetings and congratulations to the couple and their family, you wait until the dinner begins. It.s polite to place the napkin on your lap while you are eating. The waiter will serve the meal and drinks. Don't put your elbows on the table. No talking with your mouth full. Use the cuttery apropriately. Leave the table if you make a call. When the meal is finished you should place your napkin to the left of your plate. Thank the hosts after the dinner party.

The Unites States of America is a big country with a large population and people going back and forth. All of us are consumers, we buy a lot of products if not all of them with packaging, we generate tons of waste materials because of it. Here, in the US, the law is very strict about littering. I find it somehow exaggerated. While driving we can see signs on the highway with messages like: "Do not litter", "No dump, $1000 fine". No one wants to be punished with a fine, so everybody behave well concerning this topic. Even people coming from another countries change their manner of acting regarding of littering. The fines here are very high, but in the other hand, if law were not as it is, people would be different, throwing trash everywhere and the beautiful places here would be horrible. Littering and disorder are propulsif for contamination and illnesses.

La Guelaguetza The most important performing art in Mxico takes place in Oaxaca, and it''s named La Guelaguetza. There's nothing like the Guelaguetza. It's The most colorful festival of dance throughout the country. The event take place in the capital of Oaxaca an outdoor esplanade built especially for this event.  Eight regions come together to show their dances and worship the goddess of corn Centeotl. The choreography is one of a kind, and the costumes are absolutely beautiful. Pretty Indian women wear traditional costumes of their regions and show the best of their cultural traditions through dance and music. When each dance finishes, dancers give tipical food to visitors What makes this unique is that each region carries his own band, of course the bands are wind and play their parts while the dancers perform their best share. The Guelaguetza is the ultimate artistic expression and tradition in Oaxaca, Mexico. You will not see a show like this beatiful anywher else. If you can visit this festival, you will not regret.

A Pastorale is a traditional play in Mexico, it was origined in the time of the viceroyalty. In this kind of plays, people represents the fight among the good and the evil at the time of birth of Jesus Christ. In Mexico it has been one of the most important traditions all around the country and even every year we have international guests from many places around the globe, only to see this performance. Nowadays this tradition has been modified by the actors and they have given to it a touch of comedy. Last year i went to a play called "Patorale of laughter" and it was really funny, all the time I was laughing and it was also a musical play, it was livened up with mexican songs such as "el mariachi loco", " cielito lindo", "el rey", among others. It was fun to attend this play and keep alive this beautiful mexican tradition.

I was born in Torreon, northern Mexico. But I have traveled, for vacation, many times to Cancun, Southern Mexico (side to side from the country). Cancun is really a paradise, the sand and the ocean is unique. Although, Cancun has 'Xcaret', a magnificient place where you will see a great show, the best show you will ever see about Mexico. Colors, lights, sounds, smells, it's a great mix that will turn up your senses, specially if you are Mexican. At the beggining of the show, there is a Mayan culture performance. You will know the 'ball game', the ones mayans used to play ages ago. The winner offered his life to the gods. It was a privilege only to the one. Subsequently, you will see the traditional dances that represents some regions of Mexico. Lights, colors, sounds and smells remains. Finally, there is the music: 'mariachi', 'redova' and 'banda sinaloense' appears. Music loud, automatically, got you inside the show. It makes you want to sing, being a mexican citizen or not. At the end of the show, I bet you will say "yes, I want to visit Mexico again", and we hope you will.

text: English in My life  Many books, newspapers, airports and air-traffic control, technology, sports, pop music and advertising have the English as the dominant language. In general, the universal language on the Internet is English. The majority of the electronically stored information around the world is in English. English is one of the easiest languages to learn and to use for its simple alphabet, easy words, short words and easy plurals. You can travel to any English speaking country without the need of having a translator. Usually, if you don't know the language your trip would be hard and maybe you wouldn't enjoy it. Nowadays in the competitive job market it is necessary to speak English. So if you learn English you will have a better chance of getting a job that pays more. Learning English will help you to communicate with relatives, in-laws or friends who speak a different language. And is also helpful if you are going to move to a different country because it is a global language. A lot of educational information is in English; therefore to have access to this material or maybe communicate with other students it is necessary to have knowledge of English.%% It is necessary to learn English if you are planning to study at a foreign university or school. Usually many educational institutions will provide you preparatory courses to improve your English language skills but you have to have at least a medium level of knowledge. So, I'm glad that I have the opportunity to study English online at Englishtown.

We have some of the most amazing performing arts. Streets are often full of musicians and dancers. Performances can be seen in the city's public squares, and theatre, churches and storefronts in addition to the city's numerous small theatres. In Mexico, we have El Palacio de las Bellas Artes opera house, its interior is an excellent example of art deco architecture. This place hosts concerts by world-class perfomers, mostly at weekends. The Teatro Metropolitan hosts an annual jazz festival and the whole of the downtown area comes alive each March with the Festival de Mxico en el Centro Hstorico. Festival de Mxico en el Centro Histrico is the city's most important performing-arts festival, presenting close to 1,000 artists from Mexico and the world. Another venue for opera, dance and theatre is the recently restored Teatro de la Cuidad. Further afield the concert halls at UNAM are busy at weekends. Major rock and pop concerts are held at the Auditorio Nacional.

text: Discussion about the October's Men's Health magazine new front-page. Starting time - 9h The meeting has started with the welcome speech from the CEO, emphasizing the excellent results from last month. After that, the CEO started with the official agenda points: the first point was to talk about the new designs and the options to be presented. The marketing chief presented three options. The marketing chief wanted to enhance the burn fat topic, but the Sales Manager disagree about that, because this matter was discussed just in the previous number. Finally, the main topic decided for October will be how to maintain your summer skin colour in autumn in big letters at the right corner under the Magazine's logo. Business world changes everyday, therefore, management is a changing and challenging task. Some of the steps you should follow in order to head succesfully a company are: to have a mission statement; to put all the stakeholders on the same page; and to keep everybody updated. To have a mission statement is very importan for an enterprise because all the team will know the main goal of the company, and therefore everybody will be working in the same direction. Once you have stablished your mission statement, it will become easy for your employees, clients, and board members to undestand how you work and which is the scope of your projects. As managing is an ongoing job, you have to maintain all people informed of how everything is going. For example, an important tool to accomplish this step could be a blog. You can post any information, and everybody else would see and if it is necessary employees would publish any comment they could have. The steps mentioned before, are so usefull and commonly used, I highly recomend y ou to use them.

It's difficult to talk about Corporate Social Responsibility because if we consider that nowadays we are living in an era of globalization and multinational corporations; most of them' obviously are focused on generating profits. However, as result of that situation, they are affecting the environment and the social welfare as well. Fortunately, at the present more and more companies are adopting CSR, although some people say that it's just a marketing device used for companies to improve reputation of their brands. Particularly, I think corporations should not be only responsible to their shareholders; its important to have ethical principles and moral to conduct business in the wider community and make good for the society, but there are powerful arguments on both sides of the issue. On the one hand, companies only are responsible to its shareholders, but on the other hand, community is letting them to do business. For that reason they should contribute to the sustainability of the environment and support the local community by taking actions based on ethical values for the benefit of society.

Crime is among the most urgent concerns in Mexico, as a Mexican I'm very worried. Mexico has experienced increasingly high crime rates, especially in major urban centers, and is repeatedly marked by violence. I consider that drug trafficking were the main problem because it is the cause of another crimes as kidnapping, violent robbery, extortion, car thefts etc. The Government has changed some laws in order to reduce the crime, for example the sentence for a kidnapper or a blackmailer is a life sentence. Another thing that the Government did was the installation of thousands of security cameras around the city and a very large expansion of the city police force nowadays Mexico City has one of the highest police officers in the world with one uniformed police officer per every one hundred citizens, with this increase, violent and petty crime rates both falling significantly despite the rise in violent crime in other parts of the country.

There are three building in Mxico taller than 200 meters, two of them are located in Mexico City and the other one in Guadalajara: Torre Mayor (Main Tower), Torre Ejecutiva Pemex (Pemex Executive Tower) and the Riu Hotel . All of the buildings are measured from the main entrance to the top and include the spires, but not the antennas. Personally, I like buildings with character, more than tall buildings of course, this doesnt apply to New York, which I consider one of the most beautiful cities in the world with all the skyscrapers. Therefore, I like  buildings like museums, with their own personality. In Monterrey, my city, there is Marco, the Contemporary Museum of Art, which, despite the impressive collection of art that it houses, is a masterpiece in itself, with the solid structures, patio, and with all the elements that take us to the colonial times. I like also a beautiful hotel located in Mexico City (Camino Real Polanco) because its architecture includes various Mexican elements such as the patios, the tall windows and ceilings, bright colors and very vivid ambiance.

Mexico City is a blend between the history buildings and the contemporary ones. Mexico is a city created for the spanish people who came here in the 15Th century. They wanted to construct here the new Spain and because of that, they built over the arquitecture of the aztecs. Nowadays we can admire, mostly in the center of the city the ruins of the Tenochtitln city (this is the name of the ancient city). In the old city there was a lot of religious temples but they were covered with catholic churches. In this century we have modern buildings in some places of the city, so like I said before you can admire both. If you come here I recomend you to take the "turibus" this is a bus which can take you to different places of the city and they explain you the history of them and you can learn a lot of it. You can down of the bus where ever you want, it has a lot of stops around the city, but I warn you, this is one of the largest cities in the world !

However it doesnt mean that everybody can do such of crimes because if you get caught, you may be fined or punished in prison up to 6 years. There is a pilot project where all the traffic police establishes a point where they stops everybody whos driving a car, then they start by testing all the drivers first, with a few questions, and if they think that theyre in an alcoholic status, finally they do the last step which is the alcohol test. Its a kind of a alcohol test level, everybody blows into this alcohol test level thing, and if you get overpass the minimum levels, you may be put in prison up to 36 hours and plus a fine valued to $400 dollars. I would define green business as the company that, in order to get economic growth, consider essential the social growth of its employees and users of its products or services, all from the sustainable use of the resources used: water, land, energy, etc... The first and last green company goal has a threefold aspect: creating economic value from social values and environmental. The green business, not only has to achieve the satisfaction of their partners or shareholders through customer satisfaction, selling more, but also it has to earn legitimately the satisfaction of every single part involved in the business activities, starting with its workers, following for its products or services and ending with the "satisfaction" of the environment it occupies, natural resources used and waste generated, care for the environment as his own. Finally, a good green company should try their best to communicate their good work and thereby help generate a sustainable consciousness that affects sustainable consumption. No, I don't go out of my way to buy green products, but I try to help the environment using the 3R's: Reduce, recycle and reuse.

The Torre Mayor is a skyscraper in Mexico City. It's the tallest building in our country with a height of 225 metres to the top floor and 55 storeys. Located at Paseo de la Reforma, it was built by Canadian-owned Reichmann International on the former location of the Cine Chapultepec.Construction work began in 1999 and was finished in late 2003. Due to Mexico City's high propensity to earthquakes, the tower incorporates several anti-earthquake measures. Torre Mayor is one of the strongest buildings on Earth in terms of earthquake resistance, being designed to withstand earthquakes measuring 8.5 on the Richter Scale. Until 2010 it was also the tallest building in Latin America, when it was surpassed by the 236 meter high Ocean Two in Panama City.Since then, multiple new buildings in Panama City have exceeded it in height and the Gran Torre Santiago in Santiago (Chile) has topped out at 300 meters. In 2003 Torre Mayor surpassed by less than one meter the 225 meter (738 ft) high towers of the Parque Central Complex in Caracas (Venezuela). The Torre Mayor was developed by Canadian businessman Paul Reichmann, who also maintains part ownership. It is also part-owned by a group of institutional investors.%% The building was designed by the architectural firms of Zeidler Partnership Architects and Executive Architects Adamson Associates Architects, both of Toronto.

GREEN BUSINESS I think green business is mainly about being responsible with the environment. However, the subject could be quite confusing, because many unethical businesses or government agencies or political parties claim to be green just for money or as a mean to get the voters favor. Environmental friendly products are the ones produced with the minimal amount of natural resources and in an efficient way. Some examples of this are: products made of recycled materials, products made using sources of renewable energy or with biodegradable packaging. I dont go out of my way to buy green products, but I do pay a little more for greener products than for conventional ones. I also try to purchase the biggest presentations to avoid in excess packaging and I take my reusable bag to the supermarket. I also prefer to wash the dishes instead of serving in plastic or paper plates, no matter how many people attend a party at my place.

topic: Hosting a group of foreign buyers
text: In my country people tend to communicate directly to one another than indirectly, another important recommendation is not to stay too close to the person, as this is considered rude because you're invading their personal space, so be aware about that. Gestures as in many others countries are used, but what I recommend, is to do a little research about the place you're going to visit. For example, the place where I live snapping your fingers it's considered very offensive, whereas in another places in the same country is considered normal. Due to diversity of cultures all over the country, many indigenous are living now in big cities, and they can communicate in their dialect and at the same time they probably can talk in Spanish and even English, so most of their cultures communicate with gestures and they  use a wide range of gestures including bad gestures and good gestures, be aware when trying to use gestures near them, as they may think that you're talking about them and they may try to appeal at you. In overall many different verbal and non-verbal communications are allowed in my town, but just be careful when doing gestures to the people.%%

text: Mr. Charles Parks: About the sales plans you aske me for, I attached a spread sheet and the indications. I divided it ont three steps: Cold calls;  each supervisor has to fill the schedule considering the clients who doesn't buy anything the last three months, make sure how the speech is used as the client's necessity and do at least five calls each day. Meetings;  tracing the calls and the commitment with the costumer, assuring the solution of the problem or given the client's expects, that's what we call "certifying the quality service" Close sales;  just you have the order, send it immediately, track it and pull it or push it any time stop on the supply chain process. I think it's a safe bet that the cost of traditional energy going to go up - making green business ideas related to saving energy and cutting energy costs also a safe bet. Building a solar panel or wind farm is a great idea, but probably out of most people's league. But that doesn't mean you can't start a green business involving renewable energy. For instance, solar water heating systems can provide significant energy cost savings (providing up to 70% of a family's hot water when properly installed, according to some distributors). In some provinces, such as Ontario, residents can get zero-interest loans or rebates for installing solar water heating systems (PowerHouse Program), making these systems even easier to sell. If this green business idea appeals to you, Natural Resources Canada's Solar Thermal information is an excellent backgrounder. Two green business ideas stem from this experience. One, as a retailer, I would definitely start stocking eco-friendly products. Two, green products would be an excellent theme for an ecommerce website.

Guanajuato, a colonial Mexico''s city. Located in the midle of Mexican Republic, this city is famous by its architecture, history, culture and artistic activities. As you arriving, its first actractives are narrow streets though tunels, and alleys. The city was builded between hills, mountains and ravines. In addition, since 17th century, there were worked some mines. At the downtown, meet the Granaditas'' Alhondiga, a kind of warehouse in the colonial age. It is a big rectangular three floors building of rocks and mortar. At this place had an important Independence battle. Actually, the building operating as the Regional History Museum. La Paz Square is the most ancient of the city. In this place are located the buildings such architecture, the most beautiful like. Point up, the Church''s Nuestra Seora de Guanajuato, of baroque style. The Municipal Goverment Palace, constructed in 19th Century. The Rul Count House, the Legislative Palace and others, all of them builded in 18th century. The architecture is baroque and neoclassic. Other buildings to see are Juarez Theater, University of Guanajuato, Diego Rivera House and its markets. The most important yearly event is the Cervantino International Festival fulfilled in October, participating distinguished artists of opera, theater, music, dance and other arts.%% I like this city and its culture.

Talking about CSR and companies is always controversial. In Mexico there are many companies that use CSR only for publicity. That means, those companies donate money on a big TV shows, make notice on news papers, and at the end they can deduct those donations from taxes, so those are not true social responsibilities. For the companies that I work make an annual event in a local communities to provide food and clothes to the people, in this case there are no news or TV, but I don't think this is well enough. In the other hand, the customer that we have has a complete program of CSR, they are interacting with the community integrating people in to the supply chain process, they are constructing schools, hospitals and providing a stable job. This is a real case of social responsibilities and commitment with community. This was not an easy task, they are working on this program for 7 years and there are still things to do for many years. This is how mining business work in my country.

RENEWABLE ENERGY I believe that the renewable energy more suitable for my city is solar power energy, since the great amount of sun we have practically all-year long. I am a believer of this, but maybe solar power energy is not widely used yet due to the initial investment that it is necessary to do. Despite the initial cost, I am firmly convinced of the great savings that we can have in the long term. Provided that we really want to be more environmentally friendly, there are ways to obtain the money for the project, such as green-credits issued by the government or you can start with half the solar panels. I know some people whom, after installing the solar panels in their houses, have always received an energy bill on the amount of zero, or even in negative numbers! It is like a dream make true: no more bills to be paid -at least regarding energy.

text: since my point of view I totally agree with free trades between the countries,but before we make a agreement we must check out some points. 1.-Level of economy between the participants: for example all the participants must have the same economic level otherwise the richest country would take advantages over the poorer countries 2.- Education: both countries need to have the same level in education on their citizens in order to leverage all advantages that offer the trade, for example, if there is a subsidy the applicant must know how to fill up the documents otherwise the request will be denied. 3.- Sectors: traders must be careful with every sector they are negotiating for example if a country is producing grains to lower cost so they need to negotiate free entrance in order to take advantage of it but on the another hand if they are producing to a higher price that the other country they must to avoid the free entrance in order to protect the branch of national production This 3 points are very important to take into account when countries want to celebrate a trade

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: Mexico has a lot of free trade agreements with many countries, almost every one reduce import and export tariffs. The most important one is called NAFTA. It's an agreement between Mxico, USA and Canada signed in 1992 and started in January 1994. In the same time, in Mexico, some people rise in arms because they considered this kind of negotiation will bring to our country a decrease in jobs, flooded with imported goods, destroy the factories and our land will depend from the external nations decisions. After 20 years operating this contract, its farthest than real what they predicted. The economy increase, the opportunities to work rise more than expected, and the risk of national economy bankrupt is lower than before. I'm sure there is a lot of thing what this agreement didn't solve as it supposed to did, like inequality incomes on the people, the disparity between social classes, and the lack of opportunities to many people, especially in towns where they don't have enough schools and financial companies who helps them to improve their possibilities.

AD CAMPAIGN FOR CENTURY WATCHES We are going to use the slogan Golf and Century. The perfect time. It will be a nationwide campaign with TV commercials in prime time with a well known golf player who will be our ambassador for this campaign. We also are going to display our brand in billboards located very close to golf courts, executive clubs and five-star hotels. Besides, weve bought a complete page in Sunday newspapers along with central pages in business magazines in order to advertise Century. Since our product doesnt imply health issues, we dont need a disclaimer. We also are perfectly aware that our product is a luxury; therefore, we are going to treat the entire campaign this way, being selective of the places, means and people associated with our product. We are certain that the campaign will be a success and we expect to receive an advertising award along with good sales of our product!

The historic architecture of Zacatecas, in Mexico, is marvelous. The city was named cultural heritage of the humanity by the UNESCO, and its well known for its old buildings;  every building has its own history and style. For example: The Cathedral, which is located over the ruins of pre-Hispanic temples, the current structure dates from 1752 and has a faade of sculpted pink stone. The best time to appreciate the face is in the late afternoon when the sun shines directly on it. This church is considered to be one of the best examples of the Mexican Churrigueresque architecture. I guess that this temple is the most photographed in the city. Just in front of the Cathedral is located the Calderon Theater, it was built in the late 1880s. The building has a French style facade and a hall with large mirrors made in Venice. The acoustic and seats distribution are excellent, you can enjoy any kind of performance. If you come someday, I have no doubt that you will fall in love of the city.

topic: Attending a seminar on stress reduction
text: A few days ago, my son and I went at my oldest high school. He was on a math competition, when we arrived I told him how pride I was about my school. He just told me how awful was the building and the local place where it was located, then I decided to compare whole to whole, my old high school versus his new one's. Old: Located at poor section in the city, Almost 10 classrooms grades from A to J, 40 to 50 students per grade, five class hour per day, there is no course cost, the teachers was so informal. Almost every student use the bus as is primary source of transportation. New: Are located in the highest economic section of the city. They have just 3 classrooms, 20 scholars per classroom. We paid a monthly contribution to upgrade and maintain the school. To get the job, the entire teachers have to make an exam and the lowest preparation level was bachelor. Almost every one goes to the school by his or father's car. Even the difference, if the student wants to improve his self , he will reach his goal no matter on it.

I really admire my father. I think he is the most succesful man I know. Even though it is not easy to pinpoint what kind of traits and habits make someone succesful, the excellent fruitage coming from his wise way to live has helped me to realize why he is who I would like to imitate. His childhood in the countryside was not easy: he had to suffer many overwhelmed adversities. But later, when he was 18 he moved to the city and got a job in a big company. He started as a common worker. That help him to get lots of practical, hand-some experience. Then, he gained the respect of his coworkers. Even more, he was worthy to receive the trust from his boss. At last, he became retired after 40 years being the most trust-worthy and beloved employee in the company.  Therefore, I wonder, what helped him to have such success? I have seen the answer all days of my life: The love for his wife and his sons moved him to get up quite early in the morning for long long time to bring something to his children's mouth. It was not the money the most important thing in his life.%% It was happiness. He got it and money too, to enjoy some of the life pleasures.

I am from to Costa Rica, it is a small and beutiful country of Center America. The Capital of Costa Rica is San Jos, but as you could imagine, it is a small city, what means that it does not have Skyscrapers. I moved to The United States and now I live in North Carolina. The last weekend I went to Charlotte and I saw amazing buildings. In this city, there are 2 skyscrapers. I am really proud of the historic architecture of Mexico because it relates different historical epochs; however there is a wonderful and harmonious contrast with the modernism and avant-garde of the modern architecture. Nowadays, we can find in Mexico City several pre-Hispanic ruins and colonial buildings as well. Mexico Citys down town is well known as the place of the palaces because it has incomparable monuments and scenic panoramas of the National Palace and the Metropolitan Cathedral which includes a baroque and spectacular neoclassical facade and five separate naves and beautiful side chapels. Metropolitan Cathedral is the highest representation of the Roman Catholic Church in Mexico. There is also a small and enchanting colonial neighbourhood named Coyoacan which is representative of the old Mexico and includes several famous museums. On the other hand, Mexico has several modern and intelligent buildings like the Soumaya Museum, Twin Towers, World Trade Center, The trousers and hundreds of skyscrapers which are a real monument to engineering. In my opinion, the most representative building in Mexico City is the Azteca stadium. It was built in 1968 however nowadays it is a vivid demonstration of Mexican's devotion for the soccer.

topic: Writing an article about a superstore
text: Bank in Paris robbed by magicians in Las Vegas- A show hold in MGM Grand at Las Vegas was the place to steal a Bank in France. Las night show at the MGM Grand at Las Vegas, Nevada, presented how the Four Knights, magicians, stolen the Capital Bank in Paris, France. With more than five hundred persons in the show, the presenters showed all the people how they transport to Paris and draw the money out the bank. The magicians hypnotized a person from the public and sent him by teletransportation to Paris, he appeared just inside the vault, and he took the money and brought it back to the MGM Grand. But the robbery did not finish there, after the person came back through the teletransportation machine, it started raining money in the whole theater, so the audience could get some euros to take to home. FBI and Interpol are performing an investigation about the case when they found that the Capital Bank of Paris actually was stolen, so they are interviewing every one of the four magicians to solve this case and get the responsible for it. Reporting at Las Vegas, Nevada; Everardo Hernandez Sagarnaga.

I'm concerned about the enviromental warming, popullation and fosil fuels products that make damage to the earth. So I think we should use some strategies that could help our planet, and we could start with our city. We can implement solar power, if we do so, energy from the sun gets converted into thermal or electrical energy. Solar panels are used to absorb solar energy from sun's rays. Our city always has lots of sun light, and with a high quality solar panels we can have a very good energy production to our homes. We must consider the intallation and maintenance cost but if we think about it, through years we will have a great benefit about it. We could think to use Hydroelectricity or Tidal energy, but in our city we do not have water or sea to implement this type of renewable energy. Wind power sounds interesting but we need areas with strong winds and wind turbines installations, so the cost could be higher than the solar panels installation.

I live in Mexico, here the people with a bad behavior are punished! We have 2 kinds of jails, it depends of your age. If you're under 18 years old you can go to the correctional, I've heard that this kind of jail is less agressive than the adult's jail. Here we don''t have the death penalty, (electric chair,hang, lethal injection,...) no matter if the crime has been a serious one! We don''t have a court or a judge who can see the evidence neither. But we have the life impresionment !! In my particular point of view I don't think the prisoners can be reformed !!! For that I agree with the death penalty for mayor crimes: killers, violators, raptors, Why we should maintaining a person in jail who are not good to the society ?, I mean, someone who can give anything good for anyone?. Even we could be in risk, because this person could continuing commit a crime!!!. But unfortunately in my country the law hasn't been applied correctly, for that reason innocent people may be punished unfairly...

What traits and habits make a person successful? Is a hard question and I think that in order to be able to respond it, we have to define success first. But again what success means to me is not what everybody might think it is. We can have success in all areas of our live, business, academic, social, spiritual maybe in our love life or physically. And we can focus only on one type of success and work forward to reach it. However, at least for me, I prefer define success as a whole conformed by well being in all the areas that I mentioned before. I can''t imagine myself being happy with lots of money but bad health and no friends or family. The people that I consider successful have similar traits, each and every one of them have a lot of discipline and determination, discipline can make a huge difference, they also are very kind and sociable and I think the most important trait is to be passionate and stick to your believes.

The Nautilus house has one of the most unusual architecture I have ever seen. This whimsical design carries forms of aquatic life to architecture. Its forms are inspired on the nautilus shell, creating an original building, atypical and very attractive to our eyes. Completely integrated with its natural environment, this construction has spiral lines that convey the sensation of relaxing habitat. The multi-colored crystal provides a several variety of chromatic tones of the light from the outside. Furthermore, the house hasn''t internal divisions, gaining a harmonious space. It is an expression of free space, in which the main concept is that inside is an organic home. It has been constructed with ferrocement for a double purpose: first, the structure is an earthquake proof house, and second, doesn''t need any kind of additional maintenance. The Nautilus its surround by many high buildings except on the west, from where you can see a beautiful view of the mountains.

Do you want my opinion about the superstore that is about to be built in this area? Well, in my opinion there are pros and cons about it. The positive thing is that it will create new job opportunities for people around the area. Another thing is that people will not have to drive to the nearest superstore locates far away from here. In less than 5 minutes youll be there. The cons about it are the fact that in that area used to live several kinds of squirrels, birds and owls. We wont be able to see those creatures there anymore and, unfortunately, there is nothing we could do to stop this project. Also, smaller stores nearby could possibly go to bankrupt. Also the superstores will not pay as much as other places. Anyways thats what I think about it. Even so, I really love go shopping to superstores.

Mexico City is the largest city in the world. Despite its large size and its sixteen million slums, the center of Mexico City is actually very clean, and the architecture matches that of European cities. During the 1985 earthquake many of the historical buildings in the city were severely damaged. Unfortunately due to a lack of funds, and possibly a lack of government interest, many structures haven't been repaired, therefore many buildings appear to be condemned. Despite all this, every skyscraper in Mexico City is a monument to engineering. The tallest buildings in Mexico City are: with 738 ft. the Major Tower and with 801 ft. the Reforma Tower. Another taller buildings are: - Pemex Executive Tower with 693 ft. - Riu Hotel in Guadalajara with 705 ft. - Altus Tower with 640 ft. - Latinoamerican Tower with 597 ft. (this is one of the oldest buildings in Mexico City was constructed in 1956).

Mexico City is one of the most populous cities in the world. Is modern and cosmopolitan, multicultural and packed with historic sites. Mexico city is an exciting blend of Aztec, Spanish colonial and modern art and architecture. It''s also a huge, sprawling city. The Plaza de la Constitucin or Zocalo is the city''s main square. The Presidential Palace, with its famous Diego Rivera murals, is a beatiful place to visit. The surrounding Centro Historico (Historic Center) is a virtual museum of colonial architecture and was built on ruins of the Aztec capital, Tenochtitlan. The Templo Mayor, one block from the Zocalo, is the most important relic of that period. The Torre Latinoamericana is located near to the Zocalo and is one of the tallest buildings in the zone. It is an amazing building built more than fifty years ago. It has an unique observation roof that takes advantage of an equally privileged location in the heart of downtown Mexico City. We can forget the Palacio de Bellas Artes (Palace of fine arts) that is the most important cultural center in Mexico City. It is located on the west side of the Historic Center of Mexico City.

In my city home the tallest building has only 5 or 6 floors, so Id rather talk about another building. Let's talk about the tallest building in my country: La Torre Mayor. It is 230,4m tall, it has 55 floors and 13 levels to park 4 of them underground. Its construction started in 1999 and ended in 2003. This building is equipped with 98 seismic dampers and 252 piles made of steel and concret buried 60m deep, it can hold out winds of 257 Km per hour, its glasses are 2.5 cm. Thick, so it is considered one of the safest buildings in the world and the safest in Latin America. This tower is considered along with the U.S. Bank Tower, one of the world's toughest skyscrapers, and also has the most global seismic tolerance, having a maximum tolerance of 9.5 on the Richter scale, He has endured two earthquakes, one in 2003 of 7.6 degrees on the Richter scale, and in April 2007 of 6.3 degrees on the Richter scale. I'm very proud of this building, and pretty sure that you going to feel safe visiting it.

I think the green business is important for our society, because it's a good effort to reduce the climate impact. A lot of companies abuse from the interest what the people have to solve the world pollution problem and increase the price of their products making it out of the most of the people economy possibility or they lie. That's why we have to take care when we choose what we buy. Each company and each person can do many things to help on it, as minimum as they consider. Using renewable energy, solar panels for electricity and hot water, I know, some technology is expensive but cheaper than the problem it causes. Using recycled paper, even not use it, if it's not absolutely necessary. Environmentally packing, biodegradable and recycled material will be a good option. Dear Alexia, We've selected an ad campaign for Century watches. I'm sure you'll love our choice of campaign. Many people think that Century is a classic and well-known brand, because it featured some of the most famous stars in advertising campaigns. While this might be true, it's also true costumers prefer Century because it's simply the best and most reliable watchmaker on the market. That's the reason why Century has managed to be one of the top watchmakers worldwide. Considering Century's trend of working with famous stars, we contemplated the idea of bringing a celebrity into the campaign. We decided, however, that the quality of the watch was more important than celebrity endorsement. For this reason, we decided that the ad would feature a man playing golf, simple and crystal-clear, but with slogan that delivers an efficient message; a message not directed to stars and athletes, but directed to each and every man that will read an look at our ad. "Golf and Century. The perfect team. "It's a simple message that everyone can understand. I look forward to hearing from you regarding our decision. Best regards...

Technological advances are increasing continuously, so its very complicated to know how will be our life in 25 years, however taking in consideration current technology that we already have. Undoubtedly it will be integrated in most of the devices that we use every day and new discoveries and functionalities will be implemented in our homes. Probably places with major impact could be the bathroom and the kitchen because we have there many useful devices with the most potential for futuristic changes. For example, talking about the bathroom I can imagine how future toilets would be able to test urine for sugar levels, take your blood pressure or weigh and upload that information in the cloud to be sent to the doctor. Our fridge will also be able to scan our body and tell us what's inside and make a recommendation about what we should to eat or just to create a grocery list and send it to the supermarket automatically. Besides, with future advances in computing and nanotechnology we will have the opportunity of seeing new developments in medicine and science.

The skyscrapers of Mexico Mexico is a country where pre-Hispanic ruins exist in harmony with colonial buildings,  We have breathtaking  pyramids, temples, and sculptures.  If you visit Chichen-itza in Quintana Roo, you will love this magical place. With the arrival of the Spanish were introduced architectural theories of classical order and Arabic formalities, to build the first churches and monasteries  The zocalo plaza in Mexico City is 57,600 metres' (240 m  240 m), making it one of the largest city squares in the world. We also have the cathedral, is one of the oldest and largest Roman Catholic cathedral in the Americas, And its called, The Metropolitan Cathedral of the Assumption of Mary  Mexico City had its first 40-story skyscraper almost half a century ago when The Torre Lationoamericana, the tallest building in Latin America at the time, was built.  Today, there are hundreds of skyscrapers challenging nature in Mexico Citys skyline. The Torre Mayor , The world trade center, and many more skyscrapers invade the skies of my beautiful Mexico..

I really admire Fusajiro Yamauchi (NINTENDOs father), for more tan 50 years his only product was a board game called Hanafuda. Afterward in 1963, his grandson attempt to make some money investing in a taxy fleet, following that he tried to managing a hotel chain, subsequently he bought a restaurant and finally, he tried to own a TV channe. he failed in all those business, but he never give up, he is very persistente and an amazingly resilient person. He got a lot of debts, but in 1970 they focussed in a new product: the console video games. In 1975 they started to produce the first video games that turned the Nintendo Company history. In 1983 was born the very first 8 bits console Nintendo Entertainment System (NES), and with it Mario Bros y Donkey Kong. I think that Fusajiro Yamauchi and his grandson knew that every failure comes with a teaching.

Elster, the company where I work, sometimes has CSR, their Corporate Social Responsibility includes to help people who has been affected during our latest storms in a place called "La Huasteca", Elster is trying to help all those people but at the same time is asking us for victuals to help to all these people. I think  this could be a Public Relations trick, maybe they could avoid taxes or just to appear as a company who donate food to people who need it. I do not know but the good thing is they are moving their position to help others. I am not sure about shareholders, maybe this is not interesting. On the other hand, the company is promoting to do exercise, because they are arranging a race where per each round that each one of us can achieve in the industrial park, the company will donate money to some societies that need it;so this is motivating to do our best to run as much as we can, it is promoting among us the exercise as well as the convivence and the desire to help others.

Crime in Mexico is a common situation, this is a consequence of drug dealer battle for territory. North area of the country has been affected by crime. Is common to read on the news about murder, kidnapping, extortion. Those major crimes are punished by jail for at least 18 years, in Mexico is prohibited capital punish like letal injection, but in my opinion in this cases will apply. Other minor crimes are not punish by the law, for example minor thief with not violence on people under 18 years, teenagers just receive an advice and their parents need to pick up them at the court. But in case that in the thief is involve weapons, murder, or violence, they will go to preventive jail, and at the age of 18 receive a sentence in some cases for the crime. Those crimes are related to the common people, but all crimes that companies and government are doing, like taxes evasion, they move millions of pesos without punishment.

Respect of the corporate social responsibility, a think it is a very important issue, that contribute to maintain a balance in some negative and positive situations that is happening in our society, this CSR, it has several aspect to analyze for example, there are some rich persons or institutions, who have foundation that contribute to fight against illness like Cancer, AIDS, and some other conditions, these foundations pay the expensive medicines to the poor people that don't have resources to pay the medicines. Other aspect is respect the care of the environment, there are some companies that affect in a negative way the environ, this want to say that affect air, earth and water, in this case the companies are committed not only take care of the environment but restore all the areas affected as a product of their activities, that's the reason because all the governments are asking for to de new projects to make an environmental impact assessment just to handle in a responsible way any project.

Lets begin for recognizing that every company does some harm to society. Therefore, it is mandatory to give back to people as a mean to compensate for such harm. A good point to start is by acting in internal issues like paying a fair salary to all the employees or promoting people to positions according to its competences and skills, no matter if there are men or women, or how handsome they are. I have heard of some companies that excel in CSR, among them we have Starbucks that purchase the coffee grains only if workers used to recollect the crop are well compensated and if the children of the workers received a healthy breakfast daily, at no charge. I have a hope: companies begin doing CSR for an economical reason, but after a while, they do that because they really care about making a difference in the world.

Chiapas governor in Mexico, Mr. Manuel Velasco said last week that is very important to work with the society and the federal government in order to establish a new educational reform to ensure that it archives the established objectives according with the president Enrique Pea Nieto. Currently Chiapas is placed as one of Mexican states that has the biggest challenge to improve the academic quality of its schools and reduce the dropout rate. Velasco said that during his administration has launched many programs to provide incentives to stay at school, as well as to improve educational programs, including support for feeding. Additionally Mr. Velasco stressed that with the new educational reform, tutors and teachers will be able to determine priorities and decide how to use economical resources provided by the federal government, resulting in major changes in the ways school system go about making policy, generating revenues, spending founds, training teachers and helping to improve the quality of education in Chiapas.

In Mexico, it doesnt matter if you are minor or an adult. Whether you commit a petty or a serious crime, you''ll always go to jail. The place you''re sent depends of how old you are. If youre under 18 years old, you''ll be sent to juvenile detention. If youre an adult youll definitely sent to prison. Also, if you commit a petty crime, youll have to pay a fine or spend a few days in prison. But if you commit a serious crime like drunk driving youll have to pay a fine of $1,200.00 or spend 36 hours under arrest. Whether is a petty crime or a serious crime judges will not erase your personal crime record. It will always be there forever. Even so, there are still companies that hire you even if you commit those crimes. They are interested on what you can do well, instead of looking about your past.

topic: Writing an article about NLP techniques
text: MercadoLibre is an e-commerce website with operations in 12 countries in Latin America where millions of users buy and sell products over the Internet . In MercadoLibre products sold small and medium enterprises, producers, manufacturers , importers, entrepreneurs , retailers, wholesalers , private individuals , dealers, etc . To sell in MercadoLibre is necessary to register with the site ( free) and complete a sale with all data details and photos of the product offered . MercadoLibre has two options for publishing : Basic ( free ) : With a higher sales commission Normal ( cost ) allows us to highlight products, sales commission . Buyers should only register on the site , or search for products and services they need to click the "buy" button . Seller and buyer receive data from its counterpart via email in order to contact and refine the transaction. Then both can qualify to tell the rest of the user community which was his experience with his counterpart , the product and the transaction . The grading system allows buyers to meet sellers path within site .

I think buying green products is a good way to help our planet; I like the kind of green businesses that give big donations for natural organizations to prevent any kind of natural disasters. Though, if we want to help to prevent natural disasters, we need to learn to identify whether the green products we buy are created by green businesses or not. Some industries use brainwashing techniques to sell their products and people don't see the truth behind them. I think the impact of this business is good for the next generations to become more friendly in this topic and more responsible for using natural resources and then, to prevent disasters such as pollution, rainforest, oil spill, acid rain and other more. I am not use to buy green products though, but maybe someday I will consider buying the products to help and provide benefits to the world and the ecosystem.

The historic architecture of Bruges, in Belgium is marvelous The city is well known for its Gothic buildings with their pointed arches and celebratory spires built in roughly between the 13 th and 16 centuries some of the most exquisite buildings are centered around Market Square the heart of the coty if you visit Bruges I RECOMENDED you stay in the Grand Hotel cassel bergh an 18 century buildings with a cnteporary wing that blends in beautiful The hotel is conveniently situated in the center of the old city Near casselbergh is the fabulous city hall the oldest city hall in Belgium the Netherlands and Luxembourg There'S ALSO THE CHURCH OF OUR LADY WHICH HOUSES A LOVELY MICHALAGELO SCULTURE and is also the tallest building in the city The spire itself is 122 meters There is the somewhat overwhelming Concergebouw a concert hall Although I"m pleased the city decided to construct something that reflects the current time the building itself is not all that inspiring ir my opinion overall however I have no doubt that will completely enjoy a trip to Bruges and learn much about world history from our unique city.
